articles.headline: Half of active equity NFOs underperformed benchmarks since 2020
articles.summary: A Business Line analysis of 275 active equity fund NFOs launched between 2020 and 2026 shows that 48% underperformed their benchmarks. Sectoral and thematic funds saw a 50% failure rate. Dividend yield and mid-cap funds were major laggards with failure rates of 80% and 73%. Despite raising ₹4.67 lakh crore through 1,187 NFOs, many new funds fail to beat established peers or index alternatives.

articles.headline: Indian airlines warn of fleet grounding and job cuts amid war
articles.summary: India-based carriers including Air India, IndiGo, and SpiceJet have warned the Civil Aviation Ministry of potential fleet groundings and workforce downsizing. Surging operating costs driven by geopolitical tensions in West Asia have doubled jet fuel prices. Airlines propose a 10-15% cut in flight operations from March 29 and seek the removal of airfare caps and reduction in airport charges.

articles.headline: Kotak Aggressive Hybrid Fund delivers 14 percent annualised return since inception
articles.summary: The Kotak Aggressive Hybrid Fund has maintained a consistent track record over 27 years, delivering a 14% annualised return. The fund maintains a high equity allocation between 69% and 80%, with a focus on strong cash-generating businesses. Five-year rolling returns show a CAGR of 18%, outperforming the category average of 16%. It is recommended for investors with a five-year horizon.

articles.headline: Muthoot Fincorp targets 600 crore rupees through new NCD issue
articles.summary: Muthoot Fincorp has launched a non-convertible debenture (NCD) issue to raise up to ₹600 crore, including a greenshoe option. Rated AA-/Positive by CRISIL, the issue offers tenors of 24 to 72 months with yields ranging from 8.7% to 9.1%. The company reported a significant loan book growth of 43.2% in 9MFY26, primarily driven by gold loans, while maintaining a capital adequacy ratio of 18.17%.

articles.headline: HSBC Mutual Fund launches Gold ETF Fund of Fund NFO
articles.summary: HSBC Mutual Fund has opened subscription for its Gold ETF Fund of Fund, which invests in units of the HSBC Gold ETF. The New Fund Offer (NFO) is open from March 19 to March 25, 2026. The scheme has a minimum subscription of ₹5,000 and carries a 1% exit load if redeemed within 15 days of allotment. There is no entry load applicable for the scheme.

articles.headline: Choice Mutual Fund introduces new Nifty Next 50 Index Fund
articles.summary: Choice Mutual Fund has launched its Nifty Next 50 Index Fund, an open-ended scheme tracking the Nifty Next 50 Total Return Index. The NFO period runs from March 19 to April 2, 2026, with a minimum subscription of ₹1,000. The fund is managed by Rochan Pattnayak and carries no entry or exit loads, replicating the performance of its underlying benchmark index.

articles.headline: Central Mine Planning and Design Institute hits market with 1,838 crore IPO
articles.summary: CMPDI, a Mini Ratna subsidiary of Coal India, has launched an IPO seeking to raise ₹1,837.84 crore through an offer-for-sale. The price band is set at ₹163-172 per share. As a specialized mining consultancy, the firm reported a revenue CAGR of 23% between FY23 and FY25. While valuation is reasonable at a P/E of 17.5x, the company faces risks from its heavy 66% revenue dependence on Coal India.

articles.headline: Navin Fluorine well-positioned for growth through segment expansion and operational leverage
articles.summary: Navin Fluorine is expected to sustain growth momentum across its Speciality Chemicals, High Performance Products, and CDMO divisions. The company reported a 32% EBITDA margin in 9MFY26 and is investing ₹237 crore in an R32 facility. While energy price volatility remains a macro risk, analysts maintain a hold call with a three-to-five-year perspective on earnings growth.

articles.headline: Navi launches fully digital zero-commission insurance for cars and two-wheelers
articles.summary: Fintech firm Navi has introduced a zero-commission motor insurance product available directly through its app. By bypassing intermediary commissions, the company aims to offer more transparent and competitive pricing. The offering covers both internal combustion engine vehicles and electric vehicles, including comprehensive, own-damage, and third-party protection plans.

articles.headline: National Restaurant Association of India reports 30 percent business losses
articles.summary: The NRAI has flagged concerns over severe commercial LPG shortages across India, resulting in estimated business losses of 25-30%. The shortage has led to shortened service hours, limited menus, and event cancellations. While the Delhi government allowed 42% supply, the NRAI is urging the Centre and state governments to grant the restaurant industry "essential" status for fuel allocation.

articles.headline: Veranda Learning receives NCLT approval to demerge JK Shah Commerce unit
articles.summary: The NCLT Chennai Bench has directed edtech firm Veranda Learning Solutions to convene a meeting of shareholders on April 24 to approve the demerger of its commerce vertical, J.K. Shah Commerce Education. The move aims to simplify the group structure and enable a sharper strategic focus on the commerce segment through a separate listed entity, following a proposal first announced in 2025.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty hit all-time highs on RBI dividend boost
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices reached record levels after the RBI announced a higher-than-expected dividend. The Sensex jumped 1,197 points to 75,418, while the Nifty 50 surpassed the 22,900 mark during the day. Banking and financial stocks led the rally, as the liquidity infusion is seen as a significant positive for the national fiscal outlook and market sentiment.

articles.headline: IndiGo reports record Q4 profit of ₹1,895 crore on strong demand
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo) posted a net profit of ₹1,895 crore for the March quarter, a 106% increase year-on-year. The airline benefited from robust travel demand and an expanded network. Revenue rose to ₹17,825 crore. Despite challenges like grounded aircraft due to engine issues, the carrier maintained a strong market share and projected further capacity growth for the next fiscal.

articles.headline: JSW Steel reports Q4 profit drop despite record production volumes
articles.summary: JSW Steel reported a 64% fall in consolidated net profit to ₹1,299 crore for the fourth quarter, primarily due to lower realizations and exceptional items. However, the company achieved its highest-ever quarterly crude steel production of 6.79 million tonnes. The board recommended a dividend of ₹7.30 per equity share for the financial year ended March 31, 2024.

articles.headline: Air India introduces pay cuts for crew exceeding BMI limits
articles.summary: Air India's new health policy, effective May 1, stipulates that cabin crew falling outside prescribed weight limits can be pulled off flights without pay. Crew members will face unannounced BMI checks and must pass functional fitness tests to return to duty. The airline stated the policy aims to ensure crew are physically capable of handling safety and emergency duties on flights.

articles.headline: Centre announces new measures to boost manufacturing sector
articles.summary: The central government has unveiled a package of new initiatives to stimulate growth in the manufacturing sector. These measures include incentives for key industries and streamlined regulatory processes.

articles.headline: RBI mulls new framework for digital lending
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is considering a new regulatory framework for digital lending platforms. The move aims to enhance transparency and consumer protection in the burgeoning digital credit market.

articles.headline: India's startups attract significant venture capital funding
articles.summary: India's startup ecosystem continues to attract substantial venture capital investment. Funding rounds in technology, e-commerce, and fintech sectors reflect investor confidence in the country's innovation landscape.

articles.headline: Defence sector boosts 'Make in India' with new initiatives
articles.summary: The defence sector is pushing forward with 'Make in India' initiatives, unveiling new indigenous defense technologies and manufacturing capabilities. This aims to reduce import dependency and foster self-reliance.

articles.headline: New industrial policy to attract foreign investment
articles.summary: A new industrial policy has been introduced to attract greater foreign investment and boost manufacturing. The policy offers incentives and streamlined approvals for industrial projects.

articles.headline: India's fintech sector sees rapid growth
articles.summary: India's financial technology (fintech) sector is experiencing a period of rapid expansion. Innovations in digital payments, lending, and investment platforms are driving this growth.

articles.headline: New textile policy to boost exports and employment
articles.summary: The government has introduced a new textile policy aimed at boosting exports and creating employment in the sector. The policy includes incentives for modernization and skill development.

articles.headline: India's pharmaceutical industry expands global reach
articles.summary: India's pharmaceutical industry is expanding its global footprint with increased exports and international collaborations. The sector's growth is driven by its robust manufacturing capabilities and R&D.

articles.headline: India's e-commerce sector experiences exponential growth
articles.summary: India's e-commerce market continues to witness exponential growth, driven by increasing internet penetration and changing consumer habits. Online retail sales have reached new highs.

articles.headline: India's technology sector shows strong investment growth
articles.summary: India's technology sector continues to attract robust investment, particularly in areas like AI, data analytics, and software development. This growth signals a strong innovation pipeline.

articles.headline: Companies urged to adopt sustainable business practices
articles.summary: Businesses are being encouraged to adopt sustainable practices and incorporate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles. This aligns with global trends towards responsible corporate citizenship.

articles.headline: India's startup funding reaches new record levels
articles.summary: Venture capital funding for Indian startups has reached unprecedented levels, indicating strong investor confidence in the nation's innovation ecosystem. Key sectors driving this include technology and e-commerce.

articles.headline: India's IT sector expands service offerings globally
articles.summary: India's Information Technology (IT) sector is expanding its global service offerings, particularly in cloud computing and data analytics. This growth reflects the sector's increasing competitiveness.

articles.headline: Government announces relief package for MSMEs
articles.summary: The government has announced a new relief package to support Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). The measures aim to boost their liquidity and operational sustainability.

articles.headline: Consumer Cos Hike Prices Amid Rising Cost of War
articles.summary: Consumer goods firms are increasing prices due to higher costs from the Iran war, which may hurt demand and erase GST cut benefits. Products like TVs and ACs now exceed pre-GST cut prices, with hikes ranging from 1-2% for cars to 15-20% for TVs. This is the second hike this year, with more expected.

articles.headline: Bling It On! Men Embrace Jewellery as Wardrobe Staple
articles.summary: Men's jewellery sales in India are rapidly increasing, driven by celebrity endorsements and a shift from occasional wear to everyday fashion. The market share tripled to 15% of the $94.14 billion Indian jewellery market in 2025. Demand for items like rings, necklaces, and brooches is rising 25-30% year-on-year.

articles.headline: Large Manufacturing Units Mull Kitchen Revamp Amid LPG Crisis
articles.summary: Large manufacturing units in India are considering transitioning their kitchens to more energy-resilient models due to the LPG shortage caused by the West Asia war. While operations haven't been disrupted yet, companies like Flex and Sodexo India are exploring alternative energy equipment like induction and electrical setups to ensure continuity.

articles.headline: Quiet Luxury: Rich Indians Seek Stories Beyond Logos
articles.summary: A growing cohort of wealthy Indians, particularly Gen Z and millennials, are drawn to 'quiet luxury' rooted in tradition, craftsmanship, and personal experience, rather than overt brand logos. Designers and chefs are responding with entry-level lines, limited editions, and immersive experiences that embody culture and narrative.

articles.headline: Across the loom: Yarn, dyes, and logistics costs surge amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: Production costs in the textile industry have risen sharply due to increases in yarn, dyes, and logistics, driven by West Asia tensions. Processing units have increased charges, and fabric and garment prices are expected to rise if the situation persists.

articles.headline: New Mangalore Port to receive at least 72,700 tonnes of LPG
articles.summary: The New Mangalore Port is set to receive 72,700 tonnes of LPG this week from two tankers. This supply is crucial for meeting domestic demand amidst ongoing energy market uncertainties.

articles.headline: Confabulations Fuel crisis hits hospitality sector in Kerala
articles.summary: Kerala's hospitality industry is facing distress due to fuel shortages, leading to restaurant closures and reverse migration of workers. The sector seeks urgent government intervention to ensure LPG supply.

articles.headline: Union govt. removes cap on airfares after 3 months
articles.summary: The Centre has withdrawn airfare caps that were imposed to curb ticket price spikes. The Ministry of Civil Aviation stated that the situation has stabilized, allowing for the removal of the caps effective March 23.

articles.headline: Flight to Vancouver returns to Delhi after seven hours in air
articles.summary: An Air India flight to Vancouver returned to Delhi after seven hours due to an aircraft not being approved for the route. The issue stemmed from a lapse in updating operational requirements.

articles.headline: Drugmakers make a beeline to unveil generic Semaglutide
articles.summary: Indian drugmakers, including Dr. Reddy’s and Sun Pharma, are launching generic versions of Semaglutide, targeting a $1 billion market. The generic versions are priced significantly lower than the innovator product.

articles.headline: Fuel-cost hike can hit travel: AI CEO
articles.summary: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson warned staff of potential travel demand squeeze due to rising fuel costs, advising tighter control on non-essential spending. The airline may trim capacity if prices remain high.

articles.headline: India’s options boom, tale of caution
articles.summary: The article discusses the rapid growth of options trading in India, its speculative nature, and SEBI's attempts to curb excessive speculation while ensuring investor protection and market stability.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka eyes Trincomalee project to ease energy woes
articles.summary: Sri Lanka plans to expedite the Trincomalee oil tank farms redevelopment project, backed by India and UAE, as a long-term solution to its energy crisis. The project aims to create an energy hub.

articles.headline: Bangladesh rushes to secure $2 bn loan amid energy crisis
articles.summary: Bangladesh is seeking around $2 billion in loans from multilateral agencies to tackle its energy security challenges amid soaring global fuel prices. Measures include curbing consumption and seeking early disbursement.

articles.headline: Air India to have strict cabin crew fitness policy from May 1
articles.summary: Air India will implement a strict cabin crew fitness policy from May 1, linking BMI norms to flying eligibility. Non-compliant crew will face loss of pay or derostering.

articles.headline: Judge finds Musk misled Twitter shareholders
articles.summary: A federal jury found Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders, causing its share price to drop. Musk may have to pay billions in damages, though his lawyers plan to appeal.

articles.headline: More airlines amid LPG woes: Trade bodies
articles.summary: Airlines may cut flights due to rising fuel costs and economic uncertainties. Trade bodies note that escalating operating costs could force airlines to adjust fares or reduce flights.

articles.headline: Generic versions of semaglutide flood market as Novo Nordisk patent expires
articles.summary: Seven major drug-makers, including Sun Pharma, Zydus Lifesciences, and Alkem, launched generic versions of semaglutide immediately after its patent expired on Friday. The drug is the active ingredient in obesity drug Wegovy and diabetes treatment Ozempic. These generic versions are priced 50-80% lower than the innovator product, with some treatments starting as low as ₹1,800 per month.

articles.headline: New Mediclaim policy launched for Central Government Health Scheme beneficiaries
articles.summary: The Paripoorna Mediclaim Ayush Bima has been launched exclusively for CGHS beneficiaries to provide additional healthcare coverage. Administered by New India Assurance, it offers floater covers of ₹10 lakh and ₹20 lakh with access to 1,500 hospitals. The policy includes 100% coverage for AYUSH treatments but requires co-payments of either 30% or 50% from the subscribers for claims.

articles.headline: Sun Pharma net profit grows 34% to ₹2,654 crore in Q4
articles.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries reported a consolidated net profit of ₹2,654 crore for Q4 FY24, up 34% from the previous year. Revenue grew 10% to ₹11,983 crore. Growth was driven by strong performance in the US specialty business and domestic formulations. The company announced a final dividend of ₹5 per share, bringing the total dividend for the year to ₹13.5.

articles.headline: Indian pharma firms launch affordable generic weight-loss semaglutide injections
articles.summary: Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy’s, and Glenmark have launched generic versions of semaglutide following the expiry of Danish firm Novo Nordisk's patent in India. Weekly treatment costs will start as low as ₹325, significantly lower than the original brands. The move targets India's high burden of type 2 diabetes and obesity, with various formats including pre-filled pens and vials.

articles.headline: AI advancements to revolutionize India's healthcare sector
articles.summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is poised to transform India's healthcare landscape. Experts predict AI-driven diagnostics, personalized treatments, and improved patient care will become more prevalent.

articles.headline: New vaccine developed for common tropical diseases
articles.summary: Researchers have announced the development of a promising new vaccine targeting several common tropical diseases prevalent in India. Clinical trials have shown high efficacy and safety.

articles.headline: Mental health awareness campaign launched nationwide
articles.summary: A comprehensive mental health awareness campaign has been launched across India. The initiative aims to destigmatize mental health issues and promote access to support services.

articles.headline: New disease surveillance system to enhance public health
articles.summary: A nationwide disease surveillance system is being implemented to strengthen public health monitoring and response capabilities. The system will enable early detection and containment of outbreaks.

articles.headline: Health ministry expands telemedicine services
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health has announced an expansion of its telemedicine services to reach remote and underserved areas. The initiative aims to improve healthcare access for all citizens.

articles.headline: India's healthcare sector adopts advanced diagnostic tools
articles.summary: India's healthcare sector is increasingly adopting advanced diagnostic tools and technologies to improve patient care and early disease detection. This includes AI-powered solutions.

articles.headline: Price Alone Won’t Push Semaglutide Sales, say Doctors
articles.summary: Drugmakers rolling out generic semaglutide in India are focusing on device innovation and patient support, not just low pricing. Companies like Sun Pharma and Dr Reddy's offer support programs, metabolic care centers, and various vial/pen formats. Cold chain integrity and education are crucial for drug efficacy and patient onboarding.

articles.headline: BP, diabetes cases surging among staff aged 20-35
articles.summary: A study reveals BP and diabetes cases are surging among employees aged 20-35, with 1 in 4 men showing abnormal HbA1c levels. This trend is influencing corporate healthcare costs and demands early diagnosis.

articles.headline: Government approves extra 20 percent LPG allocation for commercial users
articles.summary: The Centre has increased liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) allocation to commercial users by 20%, bringing the total to 50% to mitigate shortages caused by the Gulf war. This additional supply will be distributed to hotels, restaurants, and food processing units. Educational institutions and hospitals remain prioritized. Commercial consumers in urban areas are requested to switch to PNG.

articles.headline: India prepares for critical negotiations at WTO Cameroon Ministerial Conference
articles.summary: India faces significant challenges at the upcoming 14th WTO Ministerial Conference in Cameroon. Major points of contention include the permanent extension of the e-commerce moratorium, public stockholding for food security, and fishery subsidies. India is seeking to preserve policy space and protect small-scale farmers and fishers against demands from developed nations led by the US and EU.

articles.headline: Indian refiners plan to resume buying Iranian oil after US waiver
articles.summary: Indian refiners are preparing to resume purchases of Iranian oil following the temporary 30-day waiver of US sanctions. Industry sources indicate that shipments could reach Asia within three to four days. While the move helps tame crude prices spiked by the West Asia conflict, traders are awaiting specific details from Washington regarding payment terms and insurance for the shipments.

articles.headline: RBI approves record ₹2.11 lakh crore dividend for the government
articles.summary: The RBI board approved a record surplus transfer of ₹2.11 lakh crore to the central government for FY24. This exceeds the previous year’s transfer of ₹87,416 crore and government estimates of ₹1.02 lakh crore. The windfall is expected to bolster the government’s fiscal position, potentially lowering the fiscal deficit or providing room for additional infrastructure spending.

articles.headline: India and UK officials hold virtual talks to bridge FTA gaps
articles.summary: Commerce ministry officials from India and the United Kingdom held a virtual meeting to resolve outstanding issues in the proposed Free Trade Agreement. Key sticking points remain in professional services, electric vehicles, and whiskey duties. Both sides expressed commitment to concluding a deal that benefits both economies after the conclusion of the Indian general elections.

articles.headline: Civil aviation ministry withdraws temporary domestic airfare caps
articles.summary: The ministry of civil aviation has removed domestic airfare caps ranging from ₹7,500 to ₹18,000, effective March 23. The caps were initially introduced due to market instability and high fuel costs. While controls are lifted, the government warned airlines to keep pricing reasonable and transparent, threatening to reimpose limits if excessive surge pricing is detected.

articles.headline: India's economy shows robust growth despite global headwinds
articles.summary: India's economy continues to exhibit strong growth, outpacing many global economies. Despite inflation concerns and geopolitical uncertainties, domestic demand and manufacturing have remained resilient, contributing to a positive outlook.

articles.headline: Government revises foreign investment policy
articles.summary: The government has introduced revised policies for foreign direct investment (FDI) to attract more international capital. The changes are expected to boost economic activity and create employment opportunities.

articles.headline: International trade agreements boost Indian exports
articles.summary: New international trade agreements are expected to significantly boost India's export potential. These pacts aim to open new markets and enhance trade relations.

articles.headline: Government boosts digital payments ecosystem
articles.summary: The government has announced new initiatives to further strengthen and expand India's digital payments ecosystem. The focus is on promoting financial inclusion and seamless transactions.

articles.headline: New economic survey projects optimistic growth trajectory
articles.summary: A recently released economic survey projects an optimistic growth trajectory for India, citing strong domestic demand and expanding manufacturing. The survey highlights key economic indicators.

articles.headline: RBI repo rate unchanged, inflation concerns persist
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has kept its repo rate unchanged, citing persistent inflation concerns. The central bank is closely monitoring economic indicators.

articles.headline: Global economic outlook remains cautious, says report
articles.summary: A global economic report indicates a cautious outlook, with persistent inflation and geopolitical risks affecting growth prospects. India's economic resilience is highlighted.

articles.headline: India, Others in Asia Set Sights on Iran Oil After US Waives Sanctions
articles.summary: Indian and other Asian refiners plan to resume buying Iranian oil after the US temporarily waived sanctions for 30 days. The waiver applies to oil loaded by March 20 and discharged by April 19. Around 130-170 million barrels of Iranian crude are currently at sea, a crucial supply for Asia which depends on West Asia for 60% of its crude.

articles.headline: Union Govt Doubles LPG Supply for Businesses Amid Crisis
articles.summary: The Centre has doubled the LPG allocation for commercial users to 40% of their previous consumption. This move aims to ease business disruptions for restaurants, hotels, and manufacturing units. The government had initially halted commercial supplies, prioritizing households due to the West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: New GDP Series Robust, Uses Better Data: MoSPI Secy
articles.summary: Saurabh Garg, Secretary of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, stated the revised GDP series with a 2022-23 base year is robust. It incorporates double deflation, GST data, and other new sources. India’s GDP grew 7.1% in 2024-25 and is estimated to rise 7.6% in 2025-26.

articles.headline: The LPG Crunch and Importance of Diversifying Fuel Sources
articles.summary: The current fuel crisis highlights the risks of relying on a single energy source. The author discusses the historical context of 'carbonari' and argues for rediscovering diverse fuel sources like wood and charcoal. It emphasizes the need to compensate labor involved in providing these fuels and notes challenges faced by rural communities in accessing wood.

articles.headline: Goldman and JPMorgan offer hedge funds ways to short private credit
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase are offering index-based baskets allowing hedge fund clients to bet against the $1.8 trillion private credit market. The move follows growing investor redemptions and concerns over sector exposure to software companies vulnerable to AI disruption. Several large firms, including BlackRock and Morgan Stanley, have recently limited withdrawals due to high demand.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka expedites Trincomalee oil tank farm project with India-UAE support
articles.summary: Sri Lanka is moving to redevelop World War II-era oil tank farms in Trincomalee to address its energy crisis. Foreign Minister Vijitha Herath stated the government is initiating a tender process for investors in the project, which is backed by India and the UAE. The move represents a policy shift for the ruling coalition, which previously opposed foreign involvement in the strategic site.

articles.headline: Global markets await Nvidia earnings as AI trade continues
articles.summary: Global stock markets remained cautious ahead of Nvidia’s quarterly earnings report. The chipmaker is seen as a bellwether for the artificial intelligence boom. Analysts expect revenue to more than triple compared to the previous year. The results are likely to influence the direction of technology stocks worldwide, including those in the Indian IT and semiconductor space.

articles.headline: Jury finds Elon Musk liable for defrauding Twitter shareholders
articles.summary: A San Francisco jury found Elon Musk liable for deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price prior to his $2022 acquisition. The lawsuit claimed Musk misled investors through disparaging comments about the company. While absolved of some specific 'scheme' allegations, the verdict could result in billions in damages as shareholders seek compensation for financial losses.

articles.headline: Israel strikes Iranian nuclear facility as West Asia war escalates
articles.summary: Israel targeted Iran’s Natanz nuclear enrichment site with airstrikes as the regional conflict entered its fourth week. In retaliation, Tehran launched missiles at the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean. The exchange marks a significant escalation, with Iranian missiles demonstrating a greater range than previously acknowledged by international observers.

articles.headline: Iranian Navy guides Indian LPG tanker through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: An Indian tanker was granted safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz after being guided by the Iranian Navy. Following diplomatic intervention by New Delhi, the vessel followed a pre-approved route while maintaining radio contact with Iranian forces. The development comes amid heightened risks for commercial shipping in the vital energy corridor.

articles.headline: Elon Musk accused of encouraging sexual deepfakes to boost X value
articles.summary: French prosecutors have alerted US authorities to suspicions that Elon Musk encouraged the creation of sexualized deepfakes on X using the Grok AI tool. The move is alleged to be an attempt to 'artificially' boost the company's value ahead of a planned 2026 stock market listing. French authorities are already investigating X over alleged political interference and Holocaust denial content.

articles.headline: Elon Musk offers to pay US airport staff during work stoppage
articles.summary: Billionaire Elon Musk stated he would cover the paychecks of U.S. Transportation Security Administration (TSA) officers during their second unpaid work stoppage in six months. The offer follows ongoing labor issues and government shutdowns that have impacted airport security staff. Musk has previously made similar high-profile gestures during public service disruptions.

articles.headline: India-China border talks yield progress on disengagement
articles.summary: Discussions between India and China on border issues have shown progress, with both sides agreeing to further disengage troops in certain friction points. The focus remains on de-escalation and restoring peace along the Line of Actual Control.

articles.headline: Global climate summit discusses emission reduction targets
articles.summary: India participated in a global climate summit focusing on ambitious emission reduction targets. Discussions centered on climate finance, technology transfer, and cooperative action for a sustainable future.

articles.headline: India participates in international climate resilience summit
articles.summary: India actively participated in an international summit focused on building climate resilience. Discussions centered on adaptation strategies and disaster risk reduction.

articles.headline: Fog of War Envelops West Asia & World: Trump Signals Winding Down
articles.summary: US President Trump stated talks are 'winding down' for US military efforts in West Asia, claiming objectives like degrading Iran's missile capabilities and protecting allies are near completion, despite sending more troops. The conflict has caused energy prices to soar, leading the US to temporarily waive sanctions on Iranian oil.

articles.headline: War Takes Toll On Gulf Students; Sparks Fears of Lost Academic Year
articles.summary: Thousands of Class 12 students in the Middle East face an academic crisis due to the Iran-Israel-US conflict, with exams cancelled and fears of losing an academic year. The disruption affects evaluation fairness, competitive exam access, and future opportunities, particularly for CBSE-affiliated schools in the Gulf.

articles.headline: Cricket’s Children in War-Torn Beirut Find Hope and Identity
articles.summary: In Lebanon's refugee camps, children escaping war are finding hope and identity through cricket, a sport new to them. The Alsama Project, an NGO, uses cricket and education to empower over 800 refugees, 60% of whom are girls, teaching socialisation, mental strength, and providing a pathway from illiteracy to university.

articles.headline: Russian Strikes on Infra Leaves Ukraine’s North Without Power
articles.summary: Russian strikes on energy infrastructure left most of Ukraine’s northern Chernihiv region without power. A drone attack on Zaporizhzhia also killed two people and wounded six. These attacks precede expected US-Ukraine talks in Miami, as Ukraine seeks to resume suspended trilateral discussions and secure continued weapon supply.

articles.headline: US Drones in Nigeria to Support Troops Against Militants
articles.summary: The US military deployed multiple MQ-9 drones and 200 troops to Nigeria to provide training and intelligence support against Islamist militants. The drones are collecting intelligence, not carrying out airstrikes, a US official stated. This deployment, at Nigeria's request, shows US involvement in tackling insurgencies spreading across West Africa.

articles.headline: No Easy End in Sight for West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict shows no signs of abating, with Israel vowing increased attacks on Iran, which continues missile launches. Trump gives divergent signals but temporarily waived Iran oil sanctions. The conflict, which has roiled financial markets and sent energy prices soaring, has failed to topple the Iranian regime, which is consolidating around hardliners.

articles.headline: West Asia War Hits Global Sports Events
articles.summary: The war in West Asia has led to the cancellation or postponement of dozens of major global sporting events in the Gulf, including football matches, Formula One grands prix, and endurance championships. This impacts the region's strategy to diversify its economy and improve its image through sports, despite assurances that events will return post-conflict.

articles.headline: Trump mulls ending war; U.S. eases curbs on Iran oil
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump is considering winding down the war on Iran and is easing sanctions on Iranian oil shipments. He stated that securing the Strait of Hormuz is the responsibility of other nations. Tehran claims to have attacked two American bases.

articles.headline: PM speaks with Iran on water bills
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iranian President Hassan Rouhani on the occasion of Id and Nowruz. Mr. Rouhani referred to India's BRICS presidency and urged for freedom of navigation, indicating India's concern over disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Asphyxiating an island nation: Trump’s blockade on Cuba
articles.summary: Donald Trump's blockade on Cuba has severely impacted its economy, leading to electricity grid collapse and fuel shortages. The nation struggles with spoiled food and stifling heat due to U.S. sanctions.

articles.headline: Same field, two fates: Iran and Israel at South Pars
articles.summary: The conflict between Iran and Israel has highlighted energy security fragility. Israel's attack on South Pars gas field and Iran's retaliation impact global supplies, particularly for India.

articles.headline: Iran targets Israel’s nuclear facility after Natanz strike
articles.summary: Shrapnel wounded 20 in Dimona, Israel, near its nuclear facility, following alleged Iranian missile fire. The IAEA urged 'military restraint' to avoid nuclear accidents. Iran also targeted Diego Garcia base.

articles.headline: Why is Israel attacking Lebanon?
articles.summary: Israel launched a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezbollah, citing the need to dismantle its military capabilities and create a buffer zone. Hezbollah retaliates with rockets, leading to civilian casualties.

articles.headline: Diego Garcia base key to US ops across Asia
articles.summary: Diego Garcia serves as a crucial hub for US operations across West Asia, South Asia, and the Indo-Pacific. Its strategic location supports regional military operations and monitoring of China's activities.

articles.headline: UK to Cyprus: Will not use bases for attacks
articles.summary: Britain reiterated it will not use its bases in Cyprus for offensive actions in the Iran crisis. The UK government pushed back against Iranian warnings regarding American use of British bases.

articles.headline: Iran ready to help Japan ships via Hormuz
articles.summary: Tehran is willing to help Japanese ships transit the Strait of Hormuz, according to Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi. He insisted Iran was ensuring safe passage for countries not attacking it.

articles.headline: 20+ countries want to join bid to open Strait
articles.summary: More than 20 countries condemned Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz and stated they would contribute to efforts ensuring safe passage. They condemned attacks on commercial vessels and civilian infrastructure.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes simplified nomination norms for demat and mutual fund accounts
articles.summary: SEBI has issued a consultation paper to simplify the nomination process for investors. Key proposals include making nomination the default mode, allowing investors to opt-out via a simple pop-up consent, and reducing the maximum number of nominees from ten back to four. The paper also suggests using Power of Attorney for incapacitated investors rather than granting operational powers to nominees.

articles.headline: SEBI sets rules for same-day borrowing by mutual fund schemes
articles.summary: SEBI has issued a circular formalizing short-term borrowing for mutual funds to bridge timing mismatches between redemption payouts and settlement inflows. Borrowing is restricted to meeting redemption or dividend obligations and cannot be used for leverage. Crucially, any borrowing costs must be borne by the Asset Management Company (AMC) and cannot be charged to investors.

articles.headline: Divi’s Laboratories receives 570 crore rupee Income Tax draft order
articles.summary: The Income Tax Department has issued a draft order to Divi’s Laboratories proposing an addition of ₹570.51 crore to its returned income for FY23. The proposed additions relate to transfer pricing adjustments on domestic transactions and corporate tax adjustments. The Hyderabad-based company informed stock exchanges it is evaluating the order and intends to file an appeal.

articles.headline: Supreme Court judge warns Collegium failed to protect ethical judges
articles.summary: Justice Dipankar Datta stated that the Supreme Court Collegium has historically failed to support judges who acted with courage and integrity. Speaking at an SCBA conference, he warned that a lack of protection for righteous judges might discourage judicial officers from prioritizing ethics over career growth. He appealed to fellow judges to stand by colleagues to ensure independence.

articles.headline: Justice Nagarathna identifies government as India's largest generator of disputes
articles.summary: Supreme Court judge Justice BV Nagarathna described the government as the biggest generator of litigation, significantly adding to the judicial backlog. She noted that government officials often file routine appeals to avoid audit objections or political questioning rather than seeking settlements. She called for the state to act as a model litigator and exercise restraint in legal disputes.

articles.headline: New CAPF Bill seeks to standardize IPS officer deputation limits
articles.summary: The Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, is set for introduction in the Lok Sabha. The bill aims to formalize IPS deputation levels across five CAPFs and create a single legislative framework. It proposes reserving 100% of top director general posts for IPS officers, despite long-standing objections from CAPF associations regarding senior role appointments.

articles.headline: ED claims actress Ranya Rao was duped before starting gold smuggling
articles.summary: An Enforcement Directorate charge sheet alleges Kannada actress Ranya Rao and associate Tarun Raju were cheated of ₹2 crore by a Ugandan agent before they began smuggling gold from Dubai to India. The syndicate allegedly moved 127kg of gold worth ₹102 crore between 2024 and 2025. Rao is accused of using family connections to bypass customs screening at Bengaluru airport.

articles.headline: CJI calls for 50% reservation for women lawyers in government panels
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant proposed a 50% quota for women in government advocate empanelment to address gender disparity in the legal profession. Speaking in Bengaluru, he also advocated for a financial support corpus for women during early practice years and maternity leave. He emphasized the need for institutional changes like crèches to retain women in litigation.

articles.headline: Punjab to amend sacrilege laws for stricter punishment
articles.summary: Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann announced a special assembly session on April 13 to amend laws regarding the sacrilege of religious texts. The proposed legislation seeks to introduce heavy fines, property confiscation, and exemplary punishment for desecration. The government intends to prepare the draft in consultation with legal experts and religious leaders to ensure zero tolerance.

articles.headline: Key accused arrested in Rohit Shetty residence firing incident
articles.summary: Pradeep Sharma, alias Golu Pandit, was arrested from Agra in connection with the February firing outside filmmaker Rohit Shetty’s Mumbai home. Pandit allegedly coordinated the attack from Tihar Jail and guided the shooters. This brings the total number of arrests in the case to 14. Investigators are booking the suspects under the Maharashtra Control of Organised Crime Act.

articles.headline: Judge rules Sam Altman’s sister can refile sexual abuse lawsuit
articles.summary: A federal judge dismissed Annie Altman’s sexual abuse lawsuit against her brother, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, as the claims had expired. However, she was granted permission to refile under a different Missouri statute. The judge also ruled that Sam Altman could potentially countersue his sister for defamation following her public social media allegations against him.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to hear pleas against new IT rules
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear petitions challenging the constitutional validity of the new Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021. The petitions raise concerns about freedom of speech and intermediary liability.

articles.headline: Supreme Court directs action on environmental violations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered authorities to take stringent action against companies violating environmental regulations. The directive emphasizes stricter enforcement of pollution control norms.

articles.headline: Judicial reforms to expedite case resolution
articles.summary: The government is considering implementing judicial reforms aimed at speeding up the resolution of pending cases. These measures include leveraging technology and optimizing court procedures.

articles.headline: Supreme Court upholds reservation policy
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the constitutional validity of the existing reservation policy in education and employment. The verdict emphasized the need for affirmative action to ensure social equity.

articles.headline: Supreme Court clarifies 'right to be forgotten' principles
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has provided clarification on the principles of the 'right to be forgotten' in the digital age. The ruling aims to balance privacy rights with freedom of expression.

articles.headline: Parliament passes bill to strengthen cyber security
articles.summary: Parliament has passed a new bill aimed at enhancing cyber security measures across various sectors. The legislation addresses evolving digital threats and data protection.

articles.headline: Supreme Court orders measures to combat air pollution
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed authorities to implement immediate measures to combat severe air pollution in the national capital region. The order mandates stricter emission controls and public transport improvements.

articles.headline: New legislation to protect consumer rights
articles.summary: A new legislation has been introduced to strengthen consumer protection laws, providing enhanced safeguards against unfair trade practices and product safety issues.

articles.headline: Supreme Court issues directives on judicial appointments
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has issued directives regarding the process of judicial appointments, emphasizing transparency and meritocracy. The aim is to strengthen the judiciary.

articles.headline: Supreme Court guidelines on environmental impact assessment
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has issued new guidelines for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) to ensure rigorous scrutiny of development projects and their ecological consequences.

articles.headline: Supreme Court seeks action on plastic pollution
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed authorities to take concrete steps to curb plastic pollution. The court emphasized the urgent need for effective waste management and recycling initiatives.

articles.headline: Large Haul of Arms, Grenades, IEDs Recovered in Manipur Districts
articles.summary: Security forces recovered 50 improvised explosive devices (IEDs), two SLRs, one AK rifle, pistols, grenades, and communication gear across Imphal East, Imphal West, and Tengnoupal districts of Manipur. The IEDs were destroyed in situ, and the recovery also included ammunition, camouflage vests, and radio sets.

articles.headline: HC: KSFC can’t ignore guidance value while assessing property
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court declared illegal KSFC's methodology that lowered property value below guidance value for loans. The court directed KSFC to reassess applications using the sub-registrar's guidance value.

articles.headline: State cannot place ‘arbitrary ceiling’ on disability limits: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that states cannot impose arbitrary disability limits to exclude candidates. It stated the RPwD Act, 2016, only defines a threshold for reservation eligibility.

articles.headline: What did the SC say about paid maternity leave?
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that adoptive mothers are entitled to 12 weeks of paid maternity leave, regardless of the child's age. It emphasized that motherhood is not solely biological and called for examining paternity leave.

articles.headline: AI should help judges, not replace them: CJI
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Justice Surya Kant stated AI should assist judges, not replace them, emphasizing that human judgment is crucial. He addressed concerns about AI's impact on judicial accessibility and transparency.

articles.headline: BDA’s land acquisition for BSK project upheld
articles.summary: The High Court upheld the acquisition of land for the Banashankari VI Stage Layout project by BDA, dismissing petitions challenging the process. The court noted the scheme's substantial implementation.

articles.headline: 86-yr-old can live in ancestral house: HC bins son’s plea
articles.summary: Orissa HC upheld an order allowing an 86-year-old man to live on the ground floor of his ancestral home. The court reiterated laws protecting senior citizens must advance their welfare.

articles.headline: Kerala doctor’s murderer gets life term, her kin to seek death penalty
articles.summary: A court sentenced the murderer of a young house surgeon, Dr Vandana Das, to life imprisonment. Her family plans to challenge the verdict seeking the death penalty, supported by the Indian Medical Association.

articles.headline: PM Modi urges peace and maritime security in call with Iranian President
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to discuss the volatile situation in West Asia. Modi stressed the urgent need for peace, stability, and safeguarding freedom of navigation in critical shipping lanes. He condemned attacks on regional infrastructure and reiterated India's priority for the safety of its citizens residing in Iran during the ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: Election Commission notifies 58 constituencies for Phase 6 polling
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has finalized preparations for the sixth phase of polling on May 25. Over 11 crore voters are eligible to cast votes across 58 constituencies in 8 states and UTs. High-profile candidates include Union Ministers and former Chief Ministers. Security has been significantly tightened in sensitive areas of West Bengal, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee calls PM Modi and BJP government biggest infiltrators
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee launched a sharp attack on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, labeling him and the central government as the 'biggest infiltrators.' Speaking after Eid prayers, she accused the government of deleting citizens' names from rolls while maintaining ties with leaders in West Asia. She vowed to fight to protect the voting rights of people in Bengal.

articles.headline: PM Modi calls for open shipping lanes in West Asia
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to address escalating tensions and ensure regional peace. Modi condemned attacks on critical infrastructure and emphasized the importance of keeping shipping lanes, specifically the Strait of Hormuz, open for global energy security. Pezeshkian called for Brics to help halt aggression in the region.

articles.headline: AIADMK expected to contest 170 seats in Tamil Nadu NDA pact
articles.summary: Seat-sharing negotiations for the AIADMK-led NDA in Tamil Nadu have entered their final phase. Insider reports suggest AIADMK will retain approximately 160 to 170 seats to ensure a majority on its own. The BJP is likely to be allocated 30 seats, with the PMK getting 17. The alliance aims to win 210 seats total, with Edappadi K Palaniswami as the chief ministerial face.

articles.headline: Ministers and top leaders file nominations for Kerala assembly polls
articles.summary: Several high-profile candidates, including Kerala state ministers and leaders from the Congress and BJP, filed their nomination papers on Saturday. Notable filings include BJP's Rajeev Chandrasekhar and V Muraleedharan, as well as LDF ministers P Rajeev and R Bindu. The last date for nominations is March 23, with polling scheduled for April 9.

articles.headline: Election Commission seizes freebies worth ₹75 crore in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Seizures of illegal freebies, including cash, liquor, and precious metals, have reached ₹75 crore in Tamil Nadu ahead of the assembly elections. Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik reported that intensified enforcement measures are in place to curb voter inducements. The Commission has deployed hundreds of observers across 234 constituencies to ensure fair conduct.

articles.headline: Rajeev Chandrasekhar declares assets worth over ₹93 crore in affidavit
articles.summary: BJP state president Rajeev Chandrasekhar, contesting from Kerala's Nemom constituency, has declared total assets exceeding ₹93 crore. His filing includes movable assets worth ₹78.81 crore and immovable property in Bengaluru. He also disclosed liabilities of over ₹107 crore. Chandrasekhar faces competition from LDF's V Sivankutty in the upcoming April 9 polls.

articles.headline: Congress finalizes candidate list for Karnataka assembly bypolls
articles.summary: Karnataka Congress in-charge Randeep Singh Surjewala stated the party is close to announcing candidates for the upcoming bypolls in Davangere South and Bagalkote. Intensive lobbying is ongoing among aspirants. The final decision will be made by party president Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi following recommendations from state leaders CM Siddaramaiah and DK Shivakumar.

articles.headline: Punjab minister Laljit Singh Bhullar resigns over suicide case link
articles.summary: Punjab transport and jails minister Laljit Singh Bhullar resigned after his name was linked to the alleged suicide of a state warehousing official. A video surfaced purportedly showing the official accusing Bhullar of pressure and coercion regarding a tender. Bhullar denied the allegations but stepped down to facilitate a fair inquiry. Opposition leaders have called for a CBI probe.

articles.headline: Kapil Sibal criticizes India’s silence on destruction in Iran
articles.summary: Senior advocate Kapil Sibal criticized the Indian government's silence regarding attacks on Iran, warning it could damage India's future economic interests. Sibal argued that trade and manufacturing are impossible without energy security, which depends on West Asian stability. He urged the Prime Minister to take a clear stand, suggesting silence implies support for the attackers.

articles.headline: BJP names eleven more candidates for Kerala assembly elections
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party released its third list of 11 candidates for the Kerala assembly polls. New names include V Ratheesh for Peerumade and Vivek Gopan for Aruvikkara. The party has now announced 97 candidates for the 140-member assembly. Polling is set for April 9, with the party focusing on capitalizing on anti-incumbency against the current state government.

articles.headline: BJP declares two additional candidates for Assam assembly polls
articles.summary: The BJP announced Krishna Saha for the Dalgaon seat and Jiban Gogoi for Sissibargaon in the upcoming Assam assembly elections. This brings the total number of candidates fielded by the party to 90 out of 126 seats. The BJP is contesting the polls in alliance with the Asom Gana Parishad and the Bodoland People’s Front. Polling for the state will take place on April 9.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh calls for global cooperation on terrorism
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh urged the international community to cooperate in combating terrorism, emphasizing the need for a unified global strategy. He highlighted the shared threat and the importance of collective action.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates new Parliament building complex
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the new Parliament House building, a symbol of India's democratic aspirations. The modern structure features advanced facilities and a blend of traditional and contemporary architecture.

articles.headline: Farmers' unions discuss new agricultural reforms
articles.summary: Representatives of farmers' unions are holding discussions on the implications of recent agricultural reforms. The dialogues focus on enhancing farmer income and sustainable agricultural practices.

articles.headline: Political parties engage in election campaigning
articles.summary: Political parties have intensified their campaigning efforts for upcoming state elections. Rallies, public meetings, and digital outreach are key strategies being employed by various parties.

articles.headline: Panchayati Raj institutions empowered with new digital tools
articles.summary: Panchayati Raj institutions are being equipped with new digital tools to enhance their administrative efficiency and citizen engagement. The initiative aims to decentralize governance.

articles.headline: Political parties gear up for legislative sessions
articles.summary: Political parties are preparing for the upcoming legislative sessions, with key policy agendas to be debated. Discussions are expected on economic reforms and social welfare measures.

articles.headline: Political discourse intensifies ahead of by-elections
articles.summary: Political parties are actively campaigning for upcoming by-elections. The results are seen as a key indicator of public sentiment ahead of general elections.

articles.headline: Inter-state council meeting addresses development issues
articles.summary: A meeting of the Inter-State Council focused on addressing key development issues and fostering cooperation between the Centre and states. Discussions included economic growth and resource management.

articles.headline: Parliamentary committee reviews defence modernization plans
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee is reviewing the government's defence modernization plans. The discussions focus on enhancing military capabilities and indigenization of defence equipment.

articles.headline: Eid–Nowruz Call: Modi Stresses Stability in Talk with Iran Prez
articles.summary: PM Modi conveyed Eid and Nowruz greetings to Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian, calling for peace and stability in West Asia and condemning attacks on critical infrastructure. Pezeshkian proposed a regional security framework without external interference and urged BRICS to play an independent role in halting aggression against Iran.

articles.headline: Betting on Welfare Push, BJP Targets 50% Votes in Assam
articles.summary: Emboldened by a sharp rise in welfare beneficiaries, BJP in Assam targets 50% vote share in upcoming Assembly elections. Schemes like Orunodoi (₹1,250/month to 40 lakh women) and Mahila Udyamita Scheme (seed capital and grants to 33.5 lakh women) are key to their electoral strategy.

articles.headline: UBT, BJP Trade Charges Over Links With ‘Baba’ Kharat
articles.summary: Maharashtra's Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) Sena targeted Deputy CM Eknath Shinde over his links with self-proclaimed spiritual leader Ashok Kharat, accused of rape. UBT leader Uddhav Thackeray called for prosecution of political followers of such 'fraud babas', while BJP dismissed the charges as 'ridiculous'.

articles.headline: At Eid Gathering, Mamata Alleges Voter Purge by BJP
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee accused the BJP of trying to disenfranchise minorities through alleged deletions from electoral rolls during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR). Speaking at an Eid-ul-Fitr gathering, she vowed to resist divisive politics and uphold communal harmony, while BJP accused her of politicizing a religious occasion.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand Cabinet Rejig Balances Caste Math, Backs Dhami
articles.summary: Five new ministers were inducted into Pushkar Dhami’s Uttarakhand cabinet, bringing the total to 12. The reshuffle aims to balance caste and regional equations, dispel leadership change speculation, and address anti-incumbency. It includes former state president Madan Kaushik and other MLAs, signaling party backing for Dhami.

articles.headline: From Delhi to Chennai, NDA Push to Seal Deal on Seats
articles.summary: Seat-sharing talks within the NDA in Tamil Nadu intensified ahead of Assembly elections. AIADMK and BJP leaders met to finalize agreements. BJP aims to contest at least 30 seats, shortlisting 75 constituencies, focusing on areas like Coimbatore and Chennai. The reshuffle is also expected in UP.

articles.headline: Guwahati Venue for Manipur CM’s Talks with Kuki Groups
articles.summary: Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh held a meeting with Kuki groups in Guwahati, described as a confidence-building measure. The meeting follows increasing clashes between Thangkhul Nagas and Kukis in Manipur, aiming to improve the situation after past conflicts and recent violence.

articles.headline: Nepal Poll Verdict a Chance to Reset India Ties: Ex-PM Bhattarai
articles.summary: Former Nepalese PM Baburam Bhattarai stated Nepal's election results offer an opportunity to reset ties with India and boost Nepal's economy. The polls, which saw rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) rise, reflect a transformation in Nepali politics, indicating a chance for a new beginning in bilateral relations.

articles.headline: Council Chairman sets March 25 deadline for pending government replies
articles.summary: Legislative Council Chairman Basavaraj Horatti directed the government to furnish all pending replies by March 25, warning of action against officials if the deadline is missed. Many questions remain unanswered.

articles.headline: Priyank hits out at RSS after being ‘thanked’ for route march row
articles.summary: IT-BT Minister Priyank Kharge retorted to an RSS statement, saying the organization should thank Congress for teaching them about 'the law of the land'. He criticized their 'excessive force' during an eviction.

articles.headline: CPI(M) dissident to contest as Independent with Cong. help
articles.summary: CPI(M) dissident and former Kerala Minister G. Sudhakaran will contest as an 'Independent' in Alappuzha, accepting unconditional UDF support. This move challenges the ruling LDF in its stronghold.

articles.headline: Why is govt. hesitant to take position on U.S.-Israel air strikes on Iran, asks Cong.
articles.summary: Congress condemned the Modi government for failing to condemn U.S.-Israel air strikes on Iran, questioning India's stance and diplomatic initiatives. They cited economic disruptions and 'moral cowardice'.

articles.headline: Ensure no genuine voter is struck off electoral rolls: Parliamentary panel to EC
articles.summary: A Parliamentary Standing Committee urged the Election Commission to ensure fair and transparent electoral roll revisions, warning against the exclusion of genuine voters and political party interference.

articles.headline: Media bodies slam ‘excessive force’ used during UNI sealing
articles.summary: Media bodies condemned the 'excessive force' used while evicting journalists from UNI premises in Delhi. They criticized the lack of due process and the 'undue haste' in executing the court's directive.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar skips Namaz at Patna’s Gandhi Maidan for first time in 20 years
articles.summary: For the first time in 20 years, Nitish Kumar missed the Id namaz at Patna's Gandhi Maidan. He deputed his son Nishant Kumar, sparking political speculation about a shift in the state's political landscape.

articles.headline: Trinamool’s first list for Assam Assembly polls has 17 names
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress announced its first list of 17 candidates for the Assam Assembly elections. State senior vice-president Dulu Ahmed is among the key nominees, with the NPP also fielding candidates.

articles.headline: Mamata targets Modi; Suvendu raises Hindutva pitch in Bengal
articles.summary: West Bengal political parties used Id-ul-Fitr to target opponents. Mamata Banerjee criticized PM Modi's 'Hindu-Muslim narrative', while Suvendu Adhikari invoked Hindutva, praying for 'sanatanis'.

articles.headline: Tribal council chiefs enter electoral fray in Assam
articles.summary: Tribal council chiefs Tuliram Ronghang and Tankeswar Rabha are contesting Assam Assembly elections as BJP candidates. They aim to serve their communities and address issues like ST status for indigenous communities.

articles.headline: Barring of Id prayer at Jama Masjid irk Omar, Mehbooba
articles.summary: The closure of Jama Masjid for Id prayers and omission of special prayers for Iran and Palestine drew criticism from Omar Abdullah and Mehbooba Mufti. They condemned the restrictions on religious freedom.

articles.headline: Modi slams infra attacks, says ship lanes must stay open
articles.summary: PM Modi spoke to Iranian President Pezeshkian, condemning attacks on critical infrastructure in West Asia that threaten regional stability and disrupt global supply chains. Modi reiterated the importance of safeguarding freedom of navigation and ensuring shipping lanes remain open and secure.

articles.headline: No need to entertain transfer pleas, HC tells CMO
articles.summary: The High Court observed that transfer requests should not be directly entertained by the CMO, stating such matters must be handled at the departmental level. CMO's communications are recommendatory, not approvals.

articles.headline: Trump admin seeks a leaner United Nations
articles.summary: The Trump Administration is advocating for a leaner UN focused on its core mission of maintaining peace and security. The US envoy argued that decades of UN expansion have not yielded proportional results in global stability.

articles.headline: US judge rejects Trump admin curbs on Pentagon reporters
articles.summary: A US federal judge blocked the Trump administration's decision to curtail reporters' activities at the Pentagon, ruling it a violation of free speech. The ruling favored a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.

articles.headline: Prez Putin to release Tehran: Russia stands by Iran
articles.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin congratulated Iranian leaders on Nowruz, affirming Moscow's loyalty as a friend and partner to Tehran. He conveyed greetings to Supreme Leader Khamenei and President Pezeshkian.

articles.headline: B’desh PM expresses hope for strengthening bonds
articles.summary: PM Modi and Bangladesh counterpart Tarique Rahman exchanged Eid wishes, with Rahman hoping to strengthen ties and people-to-people bonds. He outlined key points for advancing relations based on dignity and mutual respect.

articles.headline: HP CM presents ₹54,928cr budget for FY 2026-27
articles.summary: Himachal Pradesh CM Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu presented a budget of Rs 54,928 crore, reflecting a reduction compared to the previous year due to loss of revenue deficit grant and increasing debt.

articles.headline: ‘Biggest infiltrator’ is outsider jibe: Was Rahul ‘imported’ in Wayanad?
articles.summary: Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee called PM Modi the 'biggest infiltrator' for his Middle East handshakes. She also raised the issue of voter roll deletions, urging protection of rights and highlighting Bengal's unity.

articles.headline: TN NDA seat pact nears close, deal likely in two days
articles.summary: AIADMK-led NDA in Tamil Nadu is close to finalizing seat-sharing talks. AIADMK may contest around 170 seats, BJP 25-28, AMMK 10, and PMK 1-3. Dhinakaran met Amit Shah to discuss strategy.

articles.headline: Four independent candidates for LDF cross-voting in RS elections
articles.summary: LDF announced four independent candidates in Kerala assembly elections, including two in Malappuram. Uncertainty remains in Thiruvananthapuram Central as LDF seeks a suitable candidate.

articles.headline: US looking to ‘wind down’ operations in Middle East: Trump
articles.summary: President Trump stated he was considering 'winding down' US military operations in the Middle East, even as the US was sending more assault ships and Marines to the region.

articles.headline: Better career for IPS officers in CAPFs: Bill to be tabled
articles.summary: The home ministry will table a bill earmarking minimum 50% IG, 67% ADG, and all DG/Spl DG posts in CAPFs for IPS deputation, aiming for operational efficiency and better Centre-state coordination.

articles.headline: ‘Not to be tolerated’
articles.summary: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann stated that any action leading to death is not tolerated. He ordered a fair probe into the suicide of a district manager, asking the minister to resign.

articles.headline: US President Trump administration goes to war, by any memes necessary
articles.summary: The Trump administration used memes featuring movie and game characters to promote its policies, including the war on Iran. Critics argue this trivializes conflict and reflects an 'anti-meaning' approach.

articles.headline: AGP fields candidate against BJP in Guwahati Central
articles.summary: Assam's AGP has fielded a candidate against BJP in Guwahati Central, creating friction within the NDA alliance. BJP candidates also faced 'outsider' allegations.

articles.headline: US President Trump administration’s efforts to overhaul the UN
articles.summary: The Trump Administration is pushing for a leaner UN focused on peace and security. The US envoy argued that UN expansion has not improved global stability commensurate with spending.

articles.headline: Telecom providers negotiate with Mumbai Metro for network infrastructure access
articles.summary: The Cellular Operators Association of India is in talks with Mumbai Metro Railway Ltd to set up network infrastructure along the new aqua line. Current services by Vodafone Idea and BSNL are impacted following the termination of a third-party contract with ACES. Metro authorities may offer "right of way" for joint infrastructure to resolve disputes over unviable rates for equipment space.

articles.headline: Petroleum Ministry plans fast-track city gas infrastructure amid LPG crisis
articles.summary: To counter the LPG shortage caused by the West Asia conflict, the Oil Ministry is expediting the rollout of city gas distribution (CGD) infrastructure. The plan aims to migrate consumers to piped natural gas (PNG) by reducing permit timelines from five months to 15-45 days. The government is advising central offices and industrial units to switch to PNG to reduce reliance on imported LPG.

articles.headline: Adani Energy Solutions to raise ₹12,500 crore via share sale
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions received board approval to raise up to ₹12,500 crore through a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP). The funds will be used to fund future growth projects in power transmission and smart metering. This move is part of the Adani Group's broader strategy to diversify its funding base and reduce debt across its infrastructure entities.

articles.headline: IREDA to launch subsidiary for retail green energy lending
articles.summary: The Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) plans to set up a subsidiary to cater to retail segments such as rooftop solar and electric vehicle financing. The company aims to tap into growing consumer demand for green energy. IREDA's board approved the proposal to support India's target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.

articles.headline: Indian Railways to upgrade tracks for higher speed trains
articles.summary: Indian Railways plans to upgrade its track infrastructure to enable higher speed train services. The project aims to reduce travel times and improve network efficiency across key corridors.

articles.headline: Road infrastructure projects gain momentum across states
articles.summary: Several key road infrastructure projects are progressing rapidly across different states. These developments are aimed at improving connectivity, reducing logistics costs, and boosting economic growth.

articles.headline: Technology parks to be established in Tier-2 cities
articles.summary: The government plans to set up new technology parks in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities to promote regional development. This initiative aims to create job opportunities and foster innovation outside major urban centers.

articles.headline: National highway expansion project nears completion
articles.summary: A major national highway expansion project is nearing its final stages of completion, promising improved connectivity and reduced travel times. The project involves significant upgrades to existing road networks.

articles.headline: Smart city projects achieve key implementation milestones
articles.summary: Several smart city projects across India have reached significant implementation milestones. The initiatives focus on improving urban infrastructure, technology, and citizen services.

articles.headline: High-speed rail corridor project progress reviewed
articles.summary: Progress on the high-speed rail corridor project has been reviewed, with key sections nearing completion. The initiative aims to revolutionize inter-city travel.

articles.headline: Airport expansion projects to enhance air connectivity
articles.summary: Several airport expansion projects are underway to improve air connectivity and passenger capacity. These upgrades aim to boost tourism and trade.

articles.headline: Centre plans urban renewal projects in key cities
articles.summary: The central government has proposed urban renewal projects for several key cities, aiming to modernize infrastructure and improve living conditions. The plans include sustainable development initiatives.

articles.headline: Major dam construction project enters critical phase
articles.summary: A significant dam construction project has entered a critical phase, aimed at enhancing water security and irrigation for agricultural needs. The project involves advanced engineering techniques.

articles.headline: New metro line inaugurated in major city
articles.summary: A new metro rail line has been inaugurated in a major Indian city, aiming to ease urban transportation and reduce traffic congestion. The expansion is part of infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Expansion of national highway network continues
articles.summary: The expansion of India's national highway network is progressing steadily, with new projects being initiated. This aims to improve logistics and inter-state connectivity.

articles.headline: NHAI Turns on AI Dashcams for Smarter Highway Monitoring
articles.summary: NHAI will deploy AI-powered dashcam analytics on 40,000 km of national highways to improve maintenance, road safety, and user experience. The system uses AI/ML models to identify over 30 types of defects and anomalies automatically, with weekly night surveys for comprehensive road condition assessment.

articles.headline: Cost concerns delay safer building codes for Himalayas
articles.summary: India's updated building codes for earthquake safety in the Himalayas were withdrawn due to cost concerns. Scientists and engineers are reviewing the revised standards which significantly increased hazard estimates.

articles.headline: IMD issues heatwave warning as temperatures soar in Northern India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department issued a red alert for severe heatwave conditions in Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, and Rajasthan. Temperatures touched 48 degrees Celsius in parts of Rajasthan. The IMD expects no relief for the next five days. The extreme heat has led to record peak power demand in the national capital region, crossing 8,000 MW for the first time.

articles.headline: Activists oppose Delhi government plan for themed forests in Ridge
articles.summary: Environmentalists have raised concerns over the Delhi government's plan to create themed forests across the Central and South Ridge. Critics argue that developing 'special vans' or themed plantations could cause ecological disturbances in the Capital's most sensitive green zones. Activists fear the plan may prioritize aesthetics over the natural biodiversity of the protected forest area.

articles.headline: Union Minister warns against unscientific growth in fragile Bhagirathi zone
articles.summary: Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav cautioned that unscientific development in the Bhagirathi Eco-Sensitive Zone could lead to ecological disasters. During a high-level meeting, he emphasized the need for evidence-based studies and robust monitoring. The remarks follow concerns raised by activists and politicians regarding the ecological impact of infrastructure projects in the region.

articles.headline: NPC to manage environmental audits to promote sustainable growth
articles.summary: The National Productivity Council has been tasked with managing environmental audits and capacity building to strengthen industrial governance. Under an agreement with the environment ministry, the NPC will certify and register environmental auditors. The initiative aims to enhance compliance with major laws like the Environment Protection Act and support sustainable industrial expansion.

articles.headline: Peacock and turtle carcasses found in Ramgarh Vishdhari Tiger Reserve
articles.summary: Carcasses of 12 peacocks and seven turtles were discovered in the buffer zone of Rajasthan's Ramgarh Vishdhari Tiger Reserve. Forest officials suspect the animals may have died from a disease or suffocation, though autopsies are required to confirm the cause. The remains were found by locals in the Indergarh range. Wildlife authorities are investigating the deaths.

articles.headline: Study highlights rising air pollution in Indian cities
articles.summary: A recent study has highlighted alarming levels of air pollution in major Indian cities, exceeding global safety standards. The report urges immediate policy interventions to mitigate the health and environmental impacts.

articles.headline: New solar energy policy to accelerate renewable adoption
articles.summary: The government has rolled out a new solar energy policy designed to accelerate the adoption of renewable energy. The policy includes incentives for solar power generation and grid integration.

articles.headline: India's renewable energy capacity crosses significant milestone
articles.summary: India's installed renewable energy capacity has surpassed a major milestone, demonstrating a significant shift towards sustainable power generation. The growth is driven by solar and wind energy projects.

articles.headline: India pledges increased climate action at global forum
articles.summary: At a recent international forum, India reaffirmed its commitment to climate action, pledging to further increase its renewable energy targets and reduce emissions. The nation highlighted its efforts in sustainable development.

articles.headline: Wildlife conservation efforts intensified in national parks
articles.summary: Conservation efforts for endangered wildlife species are being intensified in India's national parks and sanctuaries. This includes habitat restoration and anti-poaching measures.

articles.headline: India hosts global conference on biodiversity
articles.summary: India is hosting a major international conference on biodiversity conservation. Discussions focus on strategies to protect ecosystems and endangered species.

articles.headline: Supreme Court seeks report on forest conservation
