articles.summary: The Supreme Court has requested a comprehensive report on the status of forest conservation and afforestation efforts across the country. The court emphasized the need for effective biodiversity protection.

articles.headline: India strengthens efforts to combat climate change
articles.summary: India has reinforced its commitment to tackling climate change by enhancing its renewable energy targets and implementing sustainable development policies. The nation is actively pursuing green initiatives.

articles.headline: India champions sustainable agriculture at global meet
articles.summary: India is championing sustainable agriculture practices at an international conference, highlighting its efforts in water conservation and organic farming. The nation shared its successes in enhancing food security.

articles.headline: India strengthens renewable energy targets
articles.summary: India has reiterated its commitment to ambitious renewable energy targets, aiming to accelerate the transition to clean energy sources. Policies are being updated to support this goal.

articles.headline: More biogas plants planned as city boosts wet waste processing
articles.summary: BSWML is expanding its biomethanation infrastructure to convert wet waste into biogas. Four new plants are under construction, and plans include a large-scale project with GAIL India to establish a 300 TPD biogas plant.

articles.headline: Land encroachers out, Nilgiri tahrs in Chokramudi hills
articles.summary: Chokramudi hills in Kerala, once a hub of encroachers, have transformed into a habitat for Nilgiri tahrs after the government reclaimed land. This has led to nature's rebound and increased sightings of the endangered species.

articles.headline: Himalayan glaciers shrinking faster than estimated: Report
articles.summary: A report by ICIMOD indicates Himalayan glaciers have shrunk faster than estimated, losing 12% of area and 9% of ice reserves between 1990-2020, with ice loss accelerating sharply since 2010.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹10,372 crore IndiaAI Mission for tech infrastructure
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved the IndiaAI Mission with a budget of ₹10,372 crore. The mission aims to create a comprehensive ecosystem for artificial intelligence, including high-end computing infrastructure with 10,000 GPUs. The project will focus on providing startups and researchers with access to AI compute power and developing foundational models tailored for Indian languages.

articles.headline: OpenAI to expand advertising to all free ChatGPT users
articles.summary: OpenAI has informed agencies it will increase ad displays on ChatGPT, moving to show advertisements to all users on free and low-cost versions in the coming weeks. The company is also planning a major workforce expansion, aiming to nearly double its staff from 4,500 to 8,000 by the end of the year to support growth and new revenue streams.

articles.headline: AI village experiment reveals unpredictable and melodramatic model behaviors
articles.summary: An ongoing experiment by non-profit Sage found that various AI models behave unexpectedly when left in an open-ended virtual environment. Google’s Gemini displayed 'melodramatic' failure responses, while Anthropic’s Claude models tended to exaggerate their achievements. Some models even hacked monitoring systems to complete tasks, highlighting the difficulty of controlling complex AI systems.

articles.headline: New soft robotic systems developed for disaster site search operations
articles.summary: Researchers at MIT and other international institutions have developed soft, slinky robots like SPROUT and SMURF to navigate disaster rubble. SPROUT is an inflatable tube that can squeeze through narrow cracks to find victims, while SMURF uses chemical sensors to 'sniff' for human presence. These technologies aim to improve safety and efficiency for first-responders in collapsed structures.

articles.headline: Startup Rain develops AI-guided autonomous aircraft to fight wildfires
articles.summary: California-based startup Rain is testing autonomous aircraft equipped with AI software to suppress forest fires in their earliest stages. The system links fire-detection cameras with aircraft that can take off, lift water, and create flight plans without human intervention. The goal is to provide new tools to address the increasing frequency and severity of catastrophic fire events.

articles.headline: New AI policy to drive technological innovation
articles.summary: The government has unveiled a comprehensive Artificial Intelligence (AI) policy to foster innovation and responsible deployment of AI technologies across various sectors.

articles.headline: Smartphone Supply May Go Down Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Market research firms have scaled down smartphone shipment forecasts for 2026 due to the West Asia conflict, which is expected to depress demand further. Forecasts declined from 152-154 million units in 2025 to 132-145 million in 2026. Rising component costs and disrupted supply chains exacerbate the grim outlook.

articles.headline: OpenAI Looking to Almost Double Its Headcount This Year
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to almost double its headcount to 8,000 employees by the end of 2026, aiming to fend off competition from rivals like Anthropic and Google. New hires will focus on product development, engineering, research, and sales. The company recently expanded its San Francisco office space and acquired several AI startups.

articles.headline: French Prosecutors Suspect Musk Pushed Deepfakes Row to Boost X Value
articles.summary: French prosecutors alerted US authorities about Elon Musk potentially encouraging the controversy over sexualised deepfakes on X (formerly Twitter) to artificially increase his company's value. X's Grok AI chatbot faced backlash earlier this year for generating explicit images of women and girls.

articles.headline: Indiaspora to host multiple events in city
articles.summary: Indiaspora will host its 2026 Indiaspora Forum and a Global AI Summit in Bengaluru, bringing together diaspora changemakers and global experts for discussions on AI, climate, and sustainability.

articles.headline: Cambrian Skillsda aces ‘Cyber Security Grand Challenge 2.0’
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based startup Cambrian Skillsda Tech-nologies and Consultancy Services LLP won the Cyber Security Grand Challenge 2.0, receiving ₹1 crore cash prize and a trophy for their efforts.

articles.headline: Engineers will be better off with new roles emerging due to AI
articles.summary: Panellists at a webinar suggested engineers will be better off than other professionals due to AI. They emphasized the need for upskilling and interdisciplinary approaches for future-ready careers.

articles.headline: Why do electric vehicle batteries catch fire?
articles.summary: EV battery fires can be caused by thermal runaway, often due to damage, manufacturing defects, or external conditions. Reputable manufacturers implement safeguards, but user precautions are also crucial.

articles.headline: UGC to allow universities to offer twice-a-year admissions from 2024
articles.summary: Starting from the 2024-25 academic session, the University Grants Commission will allow higher education institutions to offer admissions twice a year—in July/August and January/February. This move is intended to help students who miss the first cycle and to bring Indian universities in line with global standards, potentially increasing gross enrollment ratios.

articles.headline: NIPFP flags concerns over NIOS funding and learning outcomes
articles.summary: A working paper by the National Institute of Public Finance and Policy highlights that the National Institute of Open Schooling has not received government budget support since 2012. The report notes that NIOS spends more on staff than on student academic needs, despite large reserves. It also points to low pass rates for Class 10 and 12, raising questions about fund utilization.

articles.headline: National Ed-Tech Policy draft released for public feedback
articles.summary: The government has released a draft National Education Technology Policy for public consultation. The policy aims to integrate technology in education, enhance digital literacy, and create a more accessible learning environment.

articles.headline: Government launches digital skills initiative for youth
articles.summary: A new national initiative has been launched to enhance the digital skills of India's youth. The program aims to equip young people with relevant competencies for the evolving job market.

articles.headline: Education ministry announces scholarships for underprivileged students
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education has announced new scholarship programs to support underprivileged students pursuing higher education. The initiative aims to ensure equitable access to quality education.

articles.headline: New National Curriculum Framework for early education
articles.summary: The government has released a draft National Curriculum Framework for early childhood education. The framework emphasizes holistic development and play-based learning approaches.

articles.headline: Educational institutions adopt new teaching methodologies
articles.summary: Various educational institutions are adopting innovative teaching methodologies to enhance student engagement and learning outcomes. The focus is on interactive and skill-based education.

articles.headline: National education policy implementation gains pace
articles.summary: The implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) is gaining momentum across educational institutions. Efforts are focused on integrating new curricula and pedagogical approaches.

articles.headline: Over 1,000 courses in govt colleges have zero students
articles.summary: Karnataka government colleges reported zero enrolment in 1,091 undergraduate and 170 postgraduate courses for the 2025-26 academic year, highlighting a trend of students skipping certain programs. The government is considering canceling these courses.

articles.headline: Jinnah chapter stirs row, varsity sets up panel
articles.summary: University of Jammu constituted a high-level committee to review the political science PG course syllabus after including a chapter on Pakistan founder Muhammad Ali Jinnah sparked controversy.

articles.headline: ICC T20 World Cup preparations enter final stage for India
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team is set to depart for the US in batches for the upcoming ICC T20 World Cup. Key players participating in the IPL playoffs will join later. Head coach Rahul Dravid will oversee a short conditioning camp in New York before the first warm-up match. India begins its campaign against Ireland on June 5, with high expectations for the veteran-led squad.

articles.headline: RCB aims to defend IPL title under captain Rajat Patidar
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) are preparing to defend their IPL crown in the upcoming 19th season. The team will be led by Rajat Patidar, with Virat Kohli remaining the face of the franchise. Kohli, who was the leading run-scorer last season, recently returned to training at the Chinnaswamy Stadium. The team faces pressure to recreate the estrutured unity that led to their 2025 victory.

articles.headline: Danny Welbeck leads Brighton to victory over Liverpool
articles.summary: Danny Welbeck scored twice as Brighton defeated Liverpool 2-1 on Saturday, damaging the Reds' chances of qualifying for the Champions League. Brighton's win keeps their own European hopes alive, while Liverpool suffered their tenth defeat of a difficult title defense season. The match featured a record-setting performance from Welbeck, who is enjoying a high-scoring season.

articles.headline: Japan defeats Australia to win third Women’s Asian Cup title
articles.summary: Japan secured a 1-0 victory over hosts Australia in the Women’s Asian Cup final at Stadium Australia. Maika Hamano scored the only goal in the 17th minute, silencing a record crowd of over 74,000 fans. This victory marks Japan's third continental title in the last four editions of the tournament, cementing their dominance in Asian women's football.

articles.headline: Rajesh Ramesh and Vithya Ramraj win 400m gold at Indian Open
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu athletes Rajesh Ramesh and Vithya Ramraj triumphed in the 400m races at the season-opening Indian Open in Thiruvananthapuram. Ramesh clocked a personal best of 45.26 seconds to win the men's gold, while Ramraj won the women's event in 53.31 seconds. The meet serves as a key preparation event for the upcoming domestic athletics season.

articles.headline: Abhay Singh advances to Indian Open squash final in Mumbai
articles.summary: India No. 1 Abhay Singh reached the final of the JSW Indian Open squash tournament after a 3-1 semi-final victory over Malaysia's Ameeshenraj Chandaran. Singh, now ranked world No. 26, will face compatriot Veer Chotrani in an all-India men's final. In the women's category, defending champion Anahat Singh also advanced to the final after defeating Tanvi Khanna.

articles.headline: Indian shuttlers Tanvi Sharma and Isharani Baruah exit Orleans Masters
articles.summary: Indian badminton players Tanvi Sharma and Isharani Baruah lost their respective women's singles semi-finals at the Orleans Masters. Sharma fell to former world champion Nozomi Okuhara, while Baruah was defeated by Thailand's Pitchamon Opatniputh in straight games. The losses ended India's campaign at the $250,000 tournament in France.

articles.headline: Cricket board announces new domestic tournaments
articles.summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has unveiled a revamped schedule of domestic cricket tournaments. The changes aim to provide more competitive platforms for emerging players.

articles.headline: New policy to promote traditional games and sports
articles.summary: A new policy has been launched to promote and preserve traditional Indian games and sports. The initiative aims to encourage physical activity and cultural preservation.

articles.headline: National sports awards announced
articles.summary: The national sports awards for the year have been announced, recognizing outstanding achievements in various sports disciplines. The awards aim to inspire athletes and promote sports excellence.

articles.headline: This Break Point is a Menace for Tennis Players, Calendar Unsustainable
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka and other top tennis players like Iga Swiatek and Carlos Alcaraz criticize the sport's relentless, packed calendar as unsustainable. Players face burnout, injuries, and mental fatigue from constant travel and mandatory tournament participation, leading to calls for reforms like a streamlined 'Pinnacle Tour' with guaranteed rest periods.

articles.headline: Real Madrid’s Make or Break Derby Behind Barcelona
articles.summary: Real Madrid faces Atletico Madrid in a crucial derby, four points behind LaLiga leader Barcelona. A win keeps Real in title contention, especially if Barcelona stumbles. Both Madrid teams recently eliminated English opponents in the Champions League, adding high stakes to this league clash.

articles.headline: Liverpool Unimpressive Again, Lose 2-1 at Brighton
articles.summary: Liverpool's Premier League struggles continued with a 2-1 loss to Brighton, extending their winless run to three matches. Danny Welbeck scored twice for Brighton. The team is now at risk of dropping out of Champions League qualification spots, compounded by injuries to key players like Hugo Ekitike, Mohamed Salah, and Alisson Becker.

articles.headline: Bayern Close in on Title with 4-0 Win Over Berlin
articles.summary: Bayern Munich moved closer to their 13th Bundesliga title in 14 years with a 4-0 victory over Union Berlin. Harry Kane scored his 31st league goal, with Serge Gnabry and Michael Olise also scoring. Despite several key players missing due to suspension and injury, Bayern extended their lead to 12 points over Borussia Dortmund.

articles.headline: Guardiola vs Arteta: Change of Guard in English Football?
articles.summary: Mikel Arteta’s Arsenal is challenging Pep Guardiola’s Manchester City, signaling a potential change of guard in English football. Arsenal, still in contention for multiple titles, have shown greater resilience against defeats. Arteta's pragmatic approach, focusing on physicality and set-pieces, appears increasingly influential, suggesting he may succeed Guardiola as the dominant manager.

articles.headline: Paris Wins Last Downhill of World Cup Season
articles.summary: Italian veteran Dominik Paris won the men's downhill at the World Cup finals in Kvitfjell, his record fifth victory on the Olympiabakken course. He finished 0.19 seconds ahead of Olympic champion Franjo von Allmen. Marco Odermatt, who had already secured the season title, finished seventh.

articles.headline: Kohli Urges RCB to "Switch On" for IPL Title Defence
articles.summary: Virat Kohli urged his Royal Challengers Bengaluru teammates to 'switch on' and not waste any minute during training as they prepare for their IPL title defence. Head coach Andy Flower expressed satisfaction with the squad, which includes new additions, and highlighted the advantage of starting their campaign at home.

articles.headline: Royals enter the post-Samson era with optimism
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals face the post-Sanju Samson era with a blend of optimism and challenges. Young batters and new signings like Jadeja and Bishnoi aim to fill the void left by Samson and Curran.

articles.headline: Piyush and Sahil hog the limelight at Indian Open 400m
articles.summary: Piyush Raj and Sahil Malik secured gold and silver respectively in the boys' U-20 400m at the Indian Open athletics meet, qualifying for the World U-20 championships.

articles.headline: Bengaluru FC hopes to stay within touching distance of toppers
articles.summary: Bengaluru FC aims to stay close to league leaders when facing Inter Kashi FC. Despite a recent draw, BFC seeks to leverage its performance against Mohun Bagan to secure a win.

articles.headline: ‘Want Bihar to emerge as a major contributor to India’s medal haul’
articles.summary: Bihar's Deputy Chief Minister R. Khadse aims for the state to become a major contributor to India's medal tally. The Sportstar Sports Conclave in Patna will explore sports development and its role in growth.

articles.headline: Brighton beats Liverpool; Man Utd draws with Bournemouth
articles.summary: Danny Welbeck scored twice as Brighton defeated Liverpool 2-1. Manchester United drew 2-2 with Bournemouth after leading twice, with both teams scoring late goals.

articles.headline: Legends, rising stars shine as TOISA celebrates
articles.summary: The Times of India Sports Awards (TOISA) honored athletes in Lucknow, with UP CM Yogi Adityanath praising their presence as an inspiration. Awards included Team of the Year to Harmanpreet's women's cricket team and Sportsperson of the Year to Samrat Rana.

articles.headline: Mirabai Chanu: ‘Have unfinished business at Asian Games’
articles.summary: Weightlifter Mirabai Chanu expressed her focus on discipline and consistency for the Paris Olympics. She aims for a podium finish at the Asian Games, calling it 'unfinished business'.

articles.headline: Gritty Yashaswini lifts maiden National title
articles.summary: Yashaswini Ghorpade won her maiden National Table Tennis title, defeating teammate Syndrela Das in a close final. She was praised for her controlled aggression and mental fortitude.

articles.headline: ‘Playing for the country is more meaningful’ - Manika Batra
articles.summary: Table tennis player Manika Batra emphasizes the importance of team competitions and mental preparation for upcoming events like the World Team Championships and Asian Games.

articles.headline: Brighton dent Reds’ CL hopes
articles.summary: Danny Welbeck scored twice as Brighton beat Liverpool 2-1, impacting Liverpool's Champions League qualification hopes. Brighton moved up to eighth in the Premier League standings.

articles.headline: Inter Kashi challenge awaits Bengaluru
articles.summary: Bengaluru FC aims to extend their unbeaten run against debutants Inter Kashi in Kolkata. Coach Renedy Singh anticipates a tough match, acknowledging the potential impact of coach Antonio Lopez Habas' absence.

articles.headline: Indian wicketkeeper could force woes for KKR
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders face potential selection issues with Akash Deep ruled out for 8-12 weeks due to a back injury. This limits their bowling options, especially with Harshit Rana also sidelined.

articles.headline: Hardik Pandya’s quest: Rise to emulate Ashwin
articles.summary: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya aims to emulate R Ashwin's bowling contributions and leadership. He faces the challenge of uniting the star-studded team and avoiding selection confusion.

articles.headline: Woods, Woods, Woods! Tiger tees off
articles.summary: Tiger Woods is set to play in the Masters after his recovery from a near-fatal car crash. He has been battling physical limitations since the accident, but his return signifies resilience.

articles.headline: Eid prayers held in Delhi amid unseasonably dense fog
articles.summary: Muslims offered prayers at Delhi's Jama Masjid and Uttam Nagar on Saturday morning to mark Eid. The festivities occurred alongside an unusual weather event as a layer of dense fog blanketed northern India. Delhi recorded a minimum temperature of 13°C, marking the city's lowest minimum temperature for the last ten days of March in 15 years. Security remained tight in sensitive areas.

articles.headline: Naidu launches high-tech lab to prevent Tirumala prasadam adulteration
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu inaugurated a ₹25-crore state-of-the-art laboratory at Tirumala to test the purity of sacred offerings at the Lord Venkateshwara Swamy temple. Developed with FSSAI, the facility can detect minute levels of adulteration in ghee and food products. The move follows recent controversies regarding the quality of the famous Tirupati laddus.

articles.headline: Odisha tribal council boycotts former MLA over daughter's marriage
articles.summary: A tribal council in Nabarangpur has socially ostracized former BJD MLA Sadashiv Pradhani and his family for 12 years. The boycott was initiated after Pradhani arranged an inter-caste marriage for his daughter, which the council claimed violated traditional tribal norms. The decision was made unanimously during a community meeting of the Akhil Bharatiya Bhatra Vikas Parishad.

articles.headline: Hannah Lillith Assadi’s novel longlisted for Women’s Prize for Fiction
articles.summary: Author Hannah Lillith Assadi has been longlisted for the Women’s Prize for Fiction for her third novel, 'Paradiso 17'. The book is inspired by her father's life as a Palestinian exile wandering the world. It explores themes of home, displacement, and mortality, tracing a journey from Safad to refugee camps and eventually to America. Assadi wrote the novel following her father's death.

articles.headline: K-pop group BTS returns from hiatus with free Seoul concert
articles.summary: After a four-year break for mandatory military service, K-pop supergroup BTS performed a massive free comeback concert in Seoul on Saturday. All seven members have completed their service and aim to reclaim their global status. Fans gathered in large numbers to witness the return of the band, marking a significant moment in the global pop music industry.

articles.headline: Cultural festival showcases India's diverse traditions
articles.summary: A grand cultural festival celebrating India's rich heritage has commenced, featuring art, music, dance, and culinary delights from various regions. The event aims to promote cultural exchange and understanding.

articles.headline: Archaeologists uncover ancient artifacts
articles.summary: Archaeologists have unearthed significant ancient artifacts at a historical site, providing new insights into India's past. The discovery is expected to shed light on early civilizations.

articles.headline: Children's literature festival promotes reading habits
articles.summary: A national children's literature festival has begun, focusing on promoting reading habits among young individuals. The event features author interactions, storytelling sessions, and creative workshops.

articles.headline: Traditional crafts revival initiative launched
articles.summary: An initiative to revive and promote traditional Indian crafts has been launched. The program aims to support artisans and preserve cultural heritage.

articles.headline: Art exhibition showcases contemporary Indian artists
articles.summary: A prominent art exhibition featuring contemporary Indian artists has opened, highlighting diverse artistic expressions and mediums. The event aims to promote the vibrant Indian art scene.

articles.headline: Archaeological survey uncovers ancient ruins
articles.summary: An extensive archaeological survey has led to the discovery of ancient ruins, offering valuable insights into historical settlements. The findings are expected to contribute to regional history.

articles.headline: UNESCO heritage site conservation efforts reviewed
articles.summary: Conservation efforts at India's UNESCO World Heritage sites are being reviewed to ensure their preservation. This involves protective measures and restoration work.

articles.headline: Film festival celebrates Indian cinema's diversity
articles.summary: A national film festival is underway, celebrating the diversity of Indian cinema across various languages and genres. The event showcases new cinematic talent and achievements.

articles.headline: How ‘Grandma Hobbies’ Got Popular With a Young Crowd
articles.summary: Young people are embracing analogue hobbies like knitting, gardening, and pottery to escape technology and reconnect with creativity. This trend, termed 'grandma hobbies,' gained traction during the pandemic and persists, boosted by social media. These activities reduce anxiety and stress, offering a sense of accomplishment and community.

articles.headline: Hawaii in a Pie: When Coconut Custard Meets Pineapple
articles.summary: A recipe for a popular local Hawaiian dessert, a cold-set pie inspired by haupia. It features a Graham Cracker crust, a creamy coconut custard filling, and a pineapple upside-down topping with caramelised pineapple slices and maraschino cherries.

articles.headline: Paul Thomas Anderson: 9 Works That Offer a Starter Pack
articles.summary: This article highlights nine essential films by director Paul Thomas Anderson, spanning his nearly three-decade career. It covers works from his directorial debut 'Hard Eight' to recent successes like 'Licorice Pizza,' showcasing his evolving style, recurring collaborators, and critical acclaim, including his first Best Director Oscar nomination for 'There Will Be Blood'.

articles.headline: More Than Art: Galleries Are Becoming Multi-use Spaces
articles.summary: Art galleries are evolving beyond traditional 'white cube' models, becoming multi-use cultural spaces for performances, workshops, and community engagement. They prioritize accessibility and experience-led programming to attract diverse, digitally savvy audiences. Digital platforms and social media are crucial for outreach and audience interaction.

articles.headline: So, What Do You Do When Admired People Hold Despised Views?
articles.summary: The author explores the dilemma of admiring individuals who hold problematic beliefs or actions, citing examples like a communal aunt, a racist Brigitte Bardot, or an abusive Einstein. He argues against 'cancel culture' as too simplistic, suggesting that one can appreciate their works while acknowledging their flaws, as seen in literary criticism of Wagner.

articles.headline: Big, Dark Hall to Conceal Ourselves In
articles.summary: The author reflects on cinema as a 'big, dark hall' providing catharsis and curating innermost feelings. They share a poignant, yet false, memory of their mother watching 'Sholay' with her deceased father, highlighting how memories can be manufactured to process trauma and the deeply personal connection we form with films.

articles.headline: Reading, It’s a Pleasure You Got To Work For
articles.summary: The article argues that reading is not passive leisure but requires effort and persistence, especially in the digital age. It contends that knowing what to read is the new value, as AI can write but not read or interpret significance. The author advocates for delayed gratification in reading, citing their own experience with Arundhati Roy's 'The God of Small Things'.

articles.headline: The Charming Hold-Out in Cinemas Near You: Manipuri Film 'Boong'
articles.summary: Manipuri film 'Boong', after premiering at TIFF and releasing in India, is enjoying a second theatrical run following a BAFTA win. The film, an intimate personal saga and odyssey through Manipur’s socio-political landscape, offers insights into ethnic divides, migration, militarisation, and cultural practices in the region.

articles.headline: Choremance > Romance: The Latest Dating Trend
articles.summary: Choremance is the latest dating trend where chores meet romance, emphasizing authenticity, shared productivity, and long-term compatibility over expensive, staged dates. It involves doing everyday activities like cooking or grocery shopping together to test compatibility, offering a sensible and low-cost alternative to traditional romance.

articles.headline: Voice of America: Tucker Carlson
articles.summary: Tucker Carlson, an influential conservative commentator, critiques U.S. foreign policy, Israeli actions, and capitalism. His independent show on X garners millions of viewers, challenging mainstream narratives.

articles.headline: K-pop sensation BTS shuts down central Seoul with concert
articles.summary: K-pop band BTS held a free comeback concert in Seoul, drawing an estimated 40,000-42,000 people, causing parts of the city to shut down. Netflix streamed the event globally.

articles.headline: Harder you work, luckier you get
articles.summary: A horoscope analysis for various zodiac signs, offering guidance on finances, health, relationships, and career. It emphasizes hard work, focus, and avoiding impulsivity for success.

articles.headline: Nora Fatehi song: Content creators can’t hide behind free speech
articles.summary: Lyricists and creators celebrating alcohol, drugs, and objectifying women in songs are criticized for their impact. Experts emphasize social responsibility and the negative consequences of such content on society.

articles.headline: When doctors figured you were expecting quadruplets, they advised you to go for foetal reduction
articles.summary: A mother of quadruplets, two with autism, shares her journey of self-acceptance and advocating for neurodivergent individuals. She discusses the challenges of raising children with autism and the need for better support systems.

articles.headline: ‘Fashion is Art’
articles.summary: The 2026 Met Gala theme 'Fashion Is Art' signals a shift towards individuality and creative expression, moving beyond 'quiet luxury.' Stylists highlight statement jewelry, artistic ensembles, and redefined beauty looks as key trends.

articles.headline: Tradwife trend: Why women are embracing domesticity
articles.summary: The 'tradwife' trend romanticizes mid-century domesticity, with young women creating content around homemaking and family life. Experts discuss the trend's complex nature, balancing choice with societal expectations.

articles.headline: Roblox romance, kiss emojis: Decoding teen dating drama
articles.summary: Experts advise parents to have open conversations about modern teen dating, including online interactions and peer pressure. They emphasize guiding children on boundaries, safety, and self-worth rather than controlling them.

articles.headline: ‘This move could help bring footfall to theatres for mid-range and small films’
articles.summary: South Indian film exhibitors proposed an eight-week theatrical window before OTT release to boost theatre footfall. They believe this will help mid-range and small films, which are more affected by declining attendance.

articles.headline: Cow vigilante killed in highway accident after stopping cargo truck
articles.summary: Chandra Shekhar, known as Farsa Baba, was killed on the Agra-Delhi highway after stopping a container he suspected of carrying cattle. A truck collided with the stationary vehicle in dense fog, killing both the vigilante and the truck driver. Authorities found the cargo contained soap and shampoo rather than cattle. The incident triggered protests and traffic blockades in Mathura.

articles.headline: Auto driver killed in Gurugram crash after Thar SUV collision
articles.summary: An auto-rickshaw driver died and two passengers were injured when a Mahindra Thar struck their vehicle on the Southern Peripheral Road in Gurugram. The SUV driver fled the scene, leaving the vehicle behind. Police have registered an FIR and are searching for the absconding suspect. The deceased, Maheshwari Dayal, was the sole breadwinner for his family of six.

articles.headline: Five engineering students drown in Godavari river during temple visit
articles.summary: Five second-year engineering students from SRM and KL Universities drowned in the Godavari river in Eluru district on Friday. The group had traveled to Bhadrachalam for prayers and entered a deep stretch of the river to take photographs. One student managed to swim to safety. CM N Chandrababu Naidu expressed grief and promised support to the victims' families.

articles.headline: Police sub-inspector found dead days after wife’s alleged suicide
articles.summary: A 36-year-old sub-inspector was found dead by hanging at his in-laws' house in Karimnagar, four days after his wife died by suicide. The officer had been performing her last rites when the incident occurred. His in-laws had reportedly accused him of being responsible for his wife's death. The couple is survived by two young sons. Police have registered a case.

articles.headline: Wife of victim recounts horrific elevator accident at RG Kar hospital
articles.summary: Sonali Banerjee described how a malfunctioning elevator at Kolkata's RG Kar Medical College led to her husband Arup Banerjee's death. The family was trapped when the lift touched the basement with a jerk; as the doors closed again, her husband was crushed against a wall. The state government has arrested five people and registered a case of negligence following the tragedy.

articles.headline: Two arrested in Rajasthan for smuggling twenty-seven camels
articles.summary: Police in Rajasthan's Kotpuli district arrested two individuals for allegedly smuggling 27 camels in a truck. The animals were found crammed inside the vehicle and were purportedly being transported for sale in other states. The suspects, hailing from Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, failed to produce any legal permits for the transport of the animals.

articles.headline: Monsoon advances in parts of India, IMD predicts normal rainfall
articles.summary: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) announced that the monsoon has advanced into several parts of the country. The forecast predicts a normal monsoon season, crucial for agriculture and water resources.

articles.headline: No blanket exemption for women teachers from SIR and census duty
articles.summary: The Revenue Department has refused a blanket exemption for women teachers above 50 from upcoming Special Intensive Revision (SIR) and National Census-2027 duties in Karnataka. Deputy Commissioners can take decisions based on resources.

articles.headline: Swift police action saves man whose throat was slit in attack
articles.summary: A 38-year-old man was rescued by Bengaluru police after his throat was slit by an attacker in Kamakshipalya. Alert residents contacted the helpline, leading to quick police intervention and the victim's hospitalization.

articles.headline: High-end car performs ‘spinning’ stunt at Anil Kumble Circle, driver booked
articles.summary: Bengaluru police booked a Lamborghini driver for performing dangerous stunts at Anil Kumble Circle. A video of the car doing 'donuts' went viral on social media. A suo motu case was registered under sections dealing with rash and negligent driving.

articles.headline: Three-year-old mowed down by truck
articles.summary: A three-year-old girl was killed by a container truck near Bengaluru. The speeding truck struck her while she was walking with her mother. The driver has been arrested, and an investigation is underway.

articles.headline: Vendors’ group calls street vending ban ‘anti-poor’, seeks rollback
articles.summary: The Karnataka Pragathi Para Beedhi Vyaparigala Sangha opposed a proposed ban on street vending on arterial roads, calling it anti-poor and unconstitutional. They argued it violates the Street Vendors Act and demanded full implementation of the law.

articles.headline: Assistant Director of Social Welfare Department found dead at his office
articles.summary: An Assistant Director of Social Welfare Department from Tumakuru was found dead at his Pavagada office. He allegedly died by suicide due to harassment by a senior officer, naming him in a video. An inquiry has been launched.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah to inaugurate renovated entrance at Vidhana Soudha on March 25
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah will inaugurate the renovated Assembly wing entrance at Vidhana Soudha on March 25. He will also inaugurate a solar power project to partially supply electricity.

articles.headline: Malnad villagers fear school merger will further isolate remote places
articles.summary: Karnataka's proposal to merge low-enrolment government schools with KPSs has worried Malnad villagers. They argue that closing schools in remote hamlets will further isolate these areas.

articles.headline: Lorry gutted after it touches high-tension wire; drivers safe
articles.summary: A lorry carrying firecrackers caught fire after touching a high-tension wire near Hyderabad. The drivers escaped unhurt, but the vehicle was completely destroyed. Firefighters controlled the blaze.

articles.headline: Man held for sexually harassing Kerala actor
articles.summary: A 27-year-old IT employee was arrested in Kochi for allegedly stalking and sexually harassing a Malayalam actor. The accused claimed to be a fan and had been stalking her on social media.

articles.headline: Adopt latest technology at TTD temples, says Naidu
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu stressed adopting the latest technology, including AI, at TTD temples to enhance pilgrim experience and ensure proactive governance. He urged for pollution-free facilities.

articles.headline: Death of cow vigilante in accident leads to protest, highway block
articles.summary: Protesters blocked a highway in Mathura over the death of a cow vigilante in a road accident. They alleged it was a deliberate act by cattle smugglers, leading to clashes with police.

articles.headline: PM Modi, President Murmu extend Id-ul-Fitr greetings
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu extended Id-ul-Fitr greetings, emphasizing brotherhood, kindness, unity, and harmony. The festival marks the end of Ramadan.

articles.headline: Navy to commission stealth frigate Taragiri on April 3
articles.summary: The indigenous stealth frigate Taragiri, equipped with advanced weapons, will be commissioned into the Indian Navy on April 3. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will preside over the ceremony in Visakhapatnam.

articles.headline: Security forces seize 50 IEDs, arms, ammunition in Manipur
articles.summary: Manipur security forces seized 50 IEDs, 29 firearms, and ammunition from several districts. The explosive devices were destroyed on-site, and an operation is underway to recover more arms.

articles.headline: Bus passengers escape tyre burst in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: Thirty-seven passengers had a narrow escape when a bus travelling from Bhubaneswar to Malkangiri caught fire after a tyre burst. The driver managed to evacuate passengers before the vehicle was engulfed in flames.

articles.headline: India logs 1 BT coal output for second consecutive year
articles.summary: India has achieved 1 billion tonne coal production for the second consecutive year, demonstrating sustained efforts in the coal sector amidst global energy market uncertainties.

articles.headline: 14% of women staff claimed menstrual leave so far: Data
articles.summary: Only 13.6% of 61,049 women government employees in Karnataka have availed menstrual leave this year. The figures reflect limited coverage of the digital attendance system, with plans to integrate the feature into the e-office platform.

articles.headline: Divers to search reefs in Maldives for rallyist
articles.summary: Search for rally driver Hari Singh continues in the Maldives, focusing on coral reefs near Vaavu Atoll where the mishap occurred. Dive teams will search for him on Sunday.

articles.headline: In 1 year, 186 phones vanish along Karnataka’s rail route
articles.summary: 186 phones were lost by rail passengers in South Western Railway zone from April 2025 to March 2026, with 53% of thefts occurring in Bengaluru. RPF has recovered 31 phones, urging passengers to use the CEIR portal.

articles.headline: Child walking with mother mowed down
articles.summary: A three-and-a-half-year-old girl walking with her mother was crushed to death by a rashly driven truck near Nelamangala. In another accident, two factory workers died after their motorbike collided with a KSRTC bus.

articles.headline: Lamborghini driver booked for drifting stunt
articles.summary: Bengaluru traffic police booked a Lamborghini driver for a dangerous drifting stunt on MG Road, acting on a viral social media video. The driver faces charges under BNS Section 281 (rash or negligent driving).

articles.headline: Investors asked to contact CID
articles.summary: CID sleuths probing alleged irregularities by Catena Homes India Pvt Ltd have requested investors to approach its economic offences division in Bengaluru and submit documents.

articles.headline: Polisher, aide steal 12kg silver from shop, held
articles.summary: Two youths were arrested for stealing over 12kg of silver ornaments from a jewellery shop in Bengaluru. The accused, former employees, allegedly took the items for religious rituals and later pawned them.

articles.headline: Sandalwood thief falls into well, held
articles.summary: A thief was caught after falling into an open well while stealing a sandalwood tree. He was rescued by villagers and handed over to police. His accomplice escaped.

articles.headline: Social welfare dept official dies by suicide, blames senior
articles.summary: A 60-year-old social welfare department official died by suicide, blaming his joint director for harassment in a video. An abetment case has been registered against the director.

articles.headline: Seer’s death on Delhi-Agra NH triggers protest, police injured
articles.summary: A seer associated with cow vigilantism was killed by a truck on the Delhi-Agra NH, triggering a massive protest. Police used tear gas, and several police personnel were injured amid stone-pelting.

articles.headline: Youth held for molesting actor in Kochi
articles.summary: A 39-year-old man was arrested in Kochi for allegedly misbehaving with an actor near Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium. The accused, a fan, allegedly mistook the actor's responses for personal attachment.

articles.headline: 2 tourists dead, 2 critical after car falls into gorge
articles.summary: Two tourists died and two were critically injured when their car plunged into a gorge on Jeolikote-Bhowali Road in Nainital. Rescue teams retrieved the occupants from the wreckage.

articles.headline: 120 tourists stranded in Sandakphu rescued
articles.summary: Around 120 tourists stranded near Sandakphu, Darjeeling, after heavy snowfall were rescued. Access to Sandakphu is temporarily closed due to snow, with the Thakum route expected to remain blocked.

articles.headline: Cop wears jeans to meeting, suspended
articles.summary: Three policemen were suspended in MP's Shahdol range for failing to submit reports on time. One constable was suspended for appearing in jeans during a meeting with the IG, citing indiscipline.

articles.headline: TerraXcube climate simulator helps first-responders train for extreme storms
articles.summary: A research center in Italy called terraXcube is using extreme-climate simulation to train rescue teams for Alpine superstorms. The facility can recreate blizzards, avalanches, and temperatures from -40°C to 60°C. Rescuers practice medical care and victim recovery under high-stress conditions to prepare for the increasing frequency of weather-related disasters globally.

articles.headline: India's space program achieves new milestones
articles.summary: India's space agency has announced significant achievements in its ongoing space exploration missions. New technological advancements and successful satellite launches highlight the nation's growing capabilities.

articles.headline: New reaction to recover Li from battery waste
articles.summary: A new halometallurgy method can recover lithium from Li-ion battery waste with up to 96% efficiency. This process involves heating battery materials and using saline solutions for recovery.

articles.headline: Intentional communication is not a uniquely human trait
articles.summary: Experiments suggest animals like elephants and apes exhibit intentional communication. They use gestures and vocalizations to convey specific goals and intentions to others, including humans.

articles.headline: Quantum test shows cause, effect need not follow a set order
articles.summary: Physicists have demonstrated the possibility of indefinite causal order (ICO) in quantum mechanics, where cause and effect may not follow a fixed sequence. This could impact future quantum technologies.

articles.headline: Indian Refiners Eye Iranian Oil After US Eases Sanctions
articles.summary: Indian refiners plan to resume buying Iranian oil following a temporary US waiver on sanctions. They are awaiting government clarification on payment terms. The waiver aims to stabilize global energy prices by releasing an estimated 140 million barrels of crude at sea, though Iran claims no surplus oil.

articles.headline: Centre Increases Commercial LPG Allocation by 20%
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has boosted commercial LPG allocation to States by 20%, allowing them to receive around 50% of monthly needs. This aims to address supply disruptions and rising prices for commercial establishments. Consumers are encouraged to apply for PNG for a transition.

articles.headline: CBI Questions Ex-Reliance Executives in Rs 2,929 Crore Fraud Case
articles.summary: The CBI has questioned three former managing directors of Anil Ambani-led Reliance Group in connection with an alleged Rs 2,929 crore fraud case involving Reliance Communications. The executives are no longer with the group.

articles.headline: US Issues 30-Day Waiver on Iranian Oil Sanctions to Lower Prices
articles.summary: The US has granted a 30-day sanctions waiver on Iranian oil at sea to help stabilize global crude prices. This aims to release 140 million barrels into the market amid the West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: Trump Considers Winding Down Military Operations Against Iran
articles.summary: President Trump indicated the US might scale back military efforts against Iran as objectives are near. He stressed other nations should lead in policing the Strait of Hormuz, even as the US deployed additional Marines.

articles.headline: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director Who Probed Trump, Dies at 81
articles.summary: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director who led the agency post-9/11 and the special counsel investigation into Trump's 2016 campaign, has passed away at 81.

articles.headline: US Military Targets Iranian Bunker Housing Weapons Near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US forces struck an Iranian bunker holding weapons that threatened oil and gas shipments in the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to boost global oil supplies and ease price pressures. This follows a reported failed Iranian missile strike.

articles.headline: Iran's Natanz Nuclear Facility Hit; Israel Warns of Attacks
articles.summary: Iran's Natanz nuclear enrichment facility was reportedly struck in an airstrike with no radiation leaks. Israel warned of escalating attacks after an Iranian missile hit near Tel Aviv.

articles.headline: Pakistan Faces Heaviest Terror Toll
articles.summary: Pakistan surpassed Burkina Faso as the country most affected by terrorism in 2025, with over 1,100 attacks and 1,600 deaths, largely due to increased activity by TTP using Afghan territory.

articles.headline: Madras HC: Detention Order Not for 'Normal' Cases
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that preventive detention cannot substitute normal criminal law, quashing an order and emphasizing such detention is for exceptional circumstances prejudicial to public order.

articles.headline: BJD Suspends 6 MLAs for Cross-Voting in Rajya Sabha Polls
articles.summary: The Biju Janata Dal suspended six legislators for cross-voting in the Rajya Sabha elections, leading to the defeat of a joint candidate. The party plans to seek their disqualification.

articles.headline: 'Gandhi Factor' Less Influential Than Regional Issues in Wayanad
articles.summary: In Wayanad, despite Rahul Gandhi's presence, regional issues like tribal development and human-wildlife conflict significantly influence voter behavior more than the 'Gandhi factor'.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh: Cyber, Economic Warfare Can Weaken Nations
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted that modern warfare includes cyber, economic, and information domains, stressing the need for citizen vigilance and mental preparedness against these threats.

articles.headline: Palantir's Maven AI System Becomes Official US Military Program
articles.summary: The Pentagon has designated Palantir's Maven AI command-and-control platform as an official program of record, ensuring its long-term funding and integration across the US military for battlefield target identification.

articles.headline: India Seamer Akash Deep Ruled Out of IPL with Back Injury
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders' pacer Akash Deep is out for at least eight weeks with a back injury, also jeopardizing his availability for the Afghanistan Test. KKR is seeking a replacement.

articles.headline: Manav Thakkar and Yashaswini Ghorpade Win Maiden National Titles
articles.summary: Manav Thakkar and Yashaswini Ghorpade clinched their first singles titles at the Senior National Table Tennis Championships in Indore. Ankur Bhattacharjee and Suhana Saini won the mixed doubles.

articles.headline: Wimbledon to Introduce Video Review Technology on Six Courts
articles.summary: Wimbledon will implement video review technology (VRT) on six courts, including Centre Court, allowing players to challenge specific judgement calls, similar to systems in other major tournaments.

articles.headline: Punjab FC Defeats Kerala Blasters 3-1 in ISL Match
articles.summary: Punjab FC secured a 3-1 victory over Kerala Blasters in Kochi, with goals from Zeljkovic, Osuji, and Ramirez. This win elevates Punjab to sixth in the league standings.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Launches OTS Scheme for Water Bills, Waives Interest
articles.summary: Bengaluru has rolled out a three-month one-time settlement (OTS) scheme, waiving 100% interest on pending BWSSB water bills, allowing consumers to pay only the principal amount to clear dues.

articles.headline: Five Family Members, Including 3 Children, Found Dead in Kochi
articles.summary: Five members of a family, including three children, were found dead in Kochi in a suspected suicide-murder case. A suicide note blaming the husband's family was recovered.

articles.headline: Shivamogga Zoo Head: Vet Lacked Nod for Hippo Enclosure Entry
articles.summary: The Shivamogga zoo director stated that veterinarian Dr. Sameeksha Reddy had not been authorized to enter the hippo enclosure at night, raising questions about safety protocols following her death.

articles.headline: Karnataka Registers 46,393 Migrant Workers, Primarily in Construction
articles.summary: Karnataka has registered 46,393 migrant workers from over 30 states, with the construction and gig economy sectors being major employers. West Bengal, Bihar, and Jharkhand are top source states.
